websiteContent.mainContent: Privacy PolicyLast Updated: November 10, 2025Welcome to Parasail! We’re glad you’re here. This Privacy Policy (this “Policy”) describes how Parasail, Inc. (“Parasail”, “we”, or “us”) collects, uses, discloses, and otherwise processes personal data (as defined below) in connection our on-demand GPU inference services located at www.parasail.io, saas.parasail.io, and api.parasail.io (collectively, our “Platform”), and related content, services, products, and other functionality offered on or through the Platform (collectively, our “Services”). We have implemented this Policy because your privacy is important to us.1. Personal DataFor the purposes of this Policy, “personal data” means any data or information that identifies, relates to, describes, is capable of being associated with, or could reasonably be linked, directly or indirectly, with a particular natural person or household, or any other data or information that constitutes “personal data”, “personal information”, or “personally identifiable information” under applicable law.2. Personal Data We Collect from You DirectlyWe will collect any personal data that you choose to share with us, or that you allow us to access as part of creating an account on the Platform or otherwise using the Service. This may include the following:Personal data associated with creating and updating your user account(s) on the Platform. This may include things like email address and name. We use this information to administer your account, make the Platform and the Service available to you, communicate with you about your account, the Platform, and the Service, and for customer support purposes.Personal data associated with collecting and/or processing payments. This may include things like name and address. Please note that we use third party payment processors, including Stripe, to process credit card payments made to us. We do not retain any personally identifiable financial information related to credit card payments, such as credit card numbers. Instead, you will provide all such information directly to the third party processor. The processor’s use of your personal data is governed by their own privacy policy.Personal data associated with receiving and responding to inquiries or communications from you. This may include things like email, name, and any other personally identifiable data you choose to include in your communications with us. We use this information to investigate and respond to your inquiries and/or to communicate with you, and to enhance the Platform and the Service.Personal data associated with receiving and responding to support requests. This may include things like email, name, and any other personally identifiable data you choose to include in your communications with us. We use this information to provide customer support to you.Personal data associated with receiving marketing communications from us. To the extent that you agree to receive marketing communications from us, you may provide us with things like your email, name, and other personally identifiable information about yourself. We use this information to manage our communications with you, and to send you information about products and services that we think may be of interest to you. If you want to stop receiving email messages or other marketing communications from us, click the “unsubscribe” link at the bottom of the email/marketing communication. Note that you cannot unsubscribe from certain Platform-related or Service-related communications (e.g., account verification, confirmations, technical notices, or legal notices).Personal data associated with feedback you choose to provide to us. This may include things like email, name, and any other personally identifiable data you choose to include in your communications with us. We will use this information to communicate with you, to conduct market research, inform our marketing and advertising activities, and otherwise improve the Platform and the Service.Personal data associated with applying for a job with us. This may include things like your contact information, demographic information, educational and work history, information you provide to us during the course of any interviews, and any other information you choose to provide to us during the course of applying for a job with us. We will use this information to as part of the process of determining whether you are a good fit to work with us at Parasail.Device and Usage Information. We may collect data about your device, and how you and your device interact with the Platform and/or the Service. For example, we may collect usage data (including things like the features of the Platform/Service you choose to use, the web pages you visit, your interactions on our website(s), and your interactions with us via email or other methods), and device, connectivity, and configuration data (including things like information about your device, the network you use to connect to the Platform/Service, data about your operating system and other software installed on your device, IP address, browser type, and referring URLs). We use this information to improve the Platform and the Service.Metadata. We may collect certain metadata – essentially, data about your data – that could be used to identify you. This may include things like statistics related to your usage of the Platform/Service (e.g., length of engagement, location, and browser type), your usage of the API token that you use to access the Platform/Service, and your large language model (LLM) token usage and time usage (e.g., what models you choose to leverage, and how may tokens you consume). We use this information to improve the Platform and the Service.3. Personal Data We Receive About You from Third PartiesWe will collect certain personal data from third parties, which we often combine with personal data we collect from you. This may include the following:Information we receive from authentication services you use to connect to the Platform or the Service. This may include things like OIDC issuer ID (e.g., GoogleID, Auth0 ID, Okta). These services will authenticate your identity and provide you with the option to share certain personal data with us, which could include your name, email address, and other information. The specific data we receive will vary based on that third party’s policies, and your privacy settings on that third party site. We use this information to administer your account, make the Platform and the Service available to you, communicate with you about your account, the Platform, and the Service, and for customer support purposes.4. How We Use the Personal Data We CollectWe use personal data to provide and improve the Platform and the Service, and to perform essential business operations. This includes operating the Services, maintaining and improving the performance of the Services, developing new features, conducting research, and providing customer support. Examples of such uses include the following:Making the Platform and Service Available to You. We use data to carry out your transactions with us, and to make the Platform and the Service available to you. In certain cases, the Platform and/or Service may include personalized features and recommendations that enhance your productivity and enjoyment, and automatically tailor your experience based on the data we have about you.Technical support. We use data to diagnose product problems, and to provide other customer care and support services.Improving the Platform and the Service. We use data to continually improve our Platform and our Service, including system administration, system security, and adding new features or capabilities. Business Operations. We use data to develop aggregate analyses and business intelligence that enable us to operate, protect, make informed decisions, and report on the performance of our business.Recruitment. If you apply for a job with us, we use your data for recruitment purposes.Promotions. We may use your data to administer promotions, surveys, or other similar activities.Improving Advertising Campaigns. We may use your data to improve our advertising campaigns, primarily in an effort to prevent targeting of impressions via third-party channels when they are not relevant to you.Sending Periodic Emails. We may use your data to send you periodic emails. Depending on the marketing preferences you select when you create your account, we may send you occasional marketing emails about our products and services, which you can unsubscribe from at any time using the link provided in the message.Generally. We use data to respond to your inquiries and requests relating to our Platform and Service, to create and administer your account, to process payments, and to provide us with information and access to resources that you have requested from us. We also use data for general business purposes, including, among other things, to improve customer service, to help us improve the content and functionality of the Platform and the Service, to better understand our users, to protect against wrongdoing, to enforce our Terms of Service, and to generally manage our business.Communications. We use data we collect to communicate with you, and to personalize our communications with you. For example, we may contact you to inform you when your subscription is ending, to discuss your account, to let you know when updates are available, to remind you about features of the Platform and the Service that are available for your use, to update you about a support request, or to invite you to participate in a survey. Additionally, you can sign up for email subscriptions, and choose whether you want to receive marketing communications from us.5. We Do Not Use Personal Data Contained in Your Input or Output to Train Our Models, or to Improve the Platform or Service.We treat personal data that is contained within Input and Output differently than in the rest of this Policy.For the purposes of this Policy, “Input” means any data, images, code, or other content (including, without limitation, text, graphics, audio files, video files, or computer software) that you either: (i) publish, upload to, or use in conjunction with the Platform; (ii) make available in conjunction with the Platform, or (iii) allow the Platform to access; and “Output” means content and/or data generated by the Platform, and/or one or more model(s), in response to a query.We will not use your Input, or your Output, or any personal data contained in that Input or Output, to train our own models or to improve the Platform or the Service.6. CookiesCookies are small files that a site or its service provider transfers to your computer’s hard drive through your web browser (if you allow it) that enables the site’s or service provider’s systems to recognize your browser and capture and remember certain information. You can choose to disable cookies, but if you do, your ability to use or access certain parts of the Platform or Service may be affected.We use cookies and other similar identifiers to understand and save your preferences for future visits, to advertise to you on other sites, and to compile aggregate data about site traffic and site interaction so that we can offer better site experiences and tools in the future. Some examples of cookies and tracking technology that may be included in the Platform and Service include Google Analytics, PostHog, and Microsoft Clarity.You may refuse to accept cookies by activating the setting on your browser that allows you to refuse the setting of cookies. However, if you choose to disable cookies, you may be unable to access certain parts of our site. A banner asking you to accept our cookies policy will be displayed upon the first visit to our website (or the first visit after you delete your cookies). Unless you have adjusted your browser setting so that it will refuse cookies and/or you have not accepted our cookies policy, our system will issue cookies when you log on to our site.Our web pages may contain electronic images known as web beacons (also called single-pixel gifs) that we use to help deliver cookies on our websites, and to count users who have visited those websites. We may also include web beacons in our promotional email messages or newsletters, to determine whether and when you open and act on them.Finally, our Services may contain web beacons or similar technologies from third-party analytics providers that help us compile aggregated statistics about the effectiveness of our promotional campaigns or other operations. These technologies enable the analytics providers to set or read their own cookies or other identifiers on your device, through which they can collect information about your online activities across applications, websites or other products. However, we prohibit these analytics providers from using web beacons on our sites to collect or access information that directly identifies you (such as your name or email address).7. How We Protect Your Personal DataThe security of your personal data is important to us. Parasail follows generally accepted industry standards, including the use of reasonable and appropriate administrative, physical, and technical safeguards, to protect your personal data. This includes implanting reasonable access controls, and limiting access to systems that contain personal data to those people who have the need to access them (e.g., the “least-privilege” principle). However, no method of transmission over the Internet, or method of electronic storage, is fully secure. Therefore, while Parasail strives to use commercially reasonable means to protect your personal data while in motion and at rest, Parasail cannot and does not guarantee your data’s absolute security or confidentiality.8. When We Share Your Information with OthersWe share your personal data with your consent, or as necessary to make the Platform and the Service available to you. We also share personal data with vendors or agents working on our behalf for the purposes described in this Policy. For example, companies we have hired to provide payment processing services, CRM services, and customer support may need access to personal data to provide those functions. In such cases, these companies are required to abide by our data privacy and security requirements, and are not allowed to use personal data they receive from us for any other purpose. We may disclose your personal data as part of a corporate transaction such as a corporate sale, merger, reorganization, dissolution, or similar event.Finally, we will access, transfer, disclose, and/or preserve personal data, when we have a good faith belief that doing so is necessary to:comply with applicable law or respond to valid legal process, judicial orders, or subpoenas; respond to requests from public or governmental authorities, including for national security or law enforcement purposes;protect the vital interests of our users, customers, or other third parties (including, for example, to prevent spam or attempts to defraud users of our products, or to help prevent the loss of life or serious injury of anyone);operate and maintain the security of the Platform and Service, including to prevent or stop an attack on our computer systems or networks; protect the rights, interests or property of Parasail or third parties; prevent or investigate possible wrongdoing in connection with the Platform of Service; or enforce our Terms of Service.We may use and share aggregated non-personal information with third parties for marketing, advertising, and analytics purposes.We do not sell your personal information to third parties.9. Where We Store and Process Personal DataPersonal data we collect may be stored and processed in the United States, the European Union, or in any other country where Parasail or its affiliates, future subsidiaries or service providers maintain facilities. The storage location(s) are chosen in order to enable efficient operation, improve performance, and create redundancies in order to protect the data in the event of an outage or other problem. We take steps to ensure that the data we collect is processed according to the provisions of this Policy, and the requirements of applicable law wherever the data is located.We transfer personal data from the European Economic Area, the UK and Switzerland to other countries, some of which have not been determined by the European Commission to have an adequate level of data protection. When we engage in such transfers, we use a variety of legal mechanisms to help ensure your rights and protections travel with your data, including standard contractual clauses that have been approved by the European Commission. 10. How Long We Retain Your InformationWe will retain your personal data for as long as necessary to make the Platform and the Service available to you, or for other essential purposes such as complying with our legal obligations, resolving disputes, and enforcing our agreements. Because these needs can vary for different types of data in the context of the different aspects of the Platform and the Service, actual retention periods can vary significantly. The criteria we use to determine the retention periods include:How long is the personal data needed to make the Platform and/or Service available, and/or operate our business? This includes such things such as maintaining and improving the performance of the Platform and the Service, keeping our systems secure, and maintaining appropriate business and financial records. This is the general rule that establishes the baseline for most data retention periods.Is there an automated control, such as in your privacy dashboard, that enables you to access and delete the personal data at any time? If there is not, a shortened data retention time will generally be adopted.Is the personal data of a sensitive type? If so, a shortened retention time would generally be appropriate.Has the user provided consent for a longer retention period? If so, we will retain the data in accordance with your consent.Is Parasail subject to a legal, contractual, or similar obligation to retain the data? Examples can include mandatory data retention laws in the applicable jurisdiction, government orders to preserve data relevant to an investigation, or data that must be retained for the purposes of litigation.We do not store or log any personal data you send as Input to the “serverless” and “dedicated” versions of the Service, and we will not inspect such data without your permission, and will only retain such data for as long as is necessary to provide the Service to you (i.e., for the time it takes to generate Output and deliver that Output to you). We generally store and log any personal data you send as Input to the “batch” version of the Service for 30 days, though the specific timeframe may vary.11. How You Can Access and Control Your Personal DataYou can view, access, edit, delete, or request a copy of your personal data for certain aspects of the Platform and the Service. You can also make choices about Parasail’s collection and use of certain data. How you can access and control your personal data will depend on which aspects of the Platform and Service you use.You can always choose whether you want to receive marketing communications from us. You can also opt out from receiving marketing communications from us by using the opt-out link on the communication.Data Access and Portability. You can request a copy of your personal data by sending an email to hello@parasail.io with “Please send me a copy of my personal data” in the subject line. Parasail will verify your ability to access that email, then send you a digital export of the data we hold that is associated with your email address. We will use reasonable efforts to respond to your request within 30 days of our receipt of the request. Data Erasure. You can request that Parasail delete your personal data by sending an email to hello@parasail.io with “Please delete my personal data” in the subject line. Parasail will verify your ability to access that email, then delete the personal data associated with your email address. We will use reasonable efforts to respond to your request within 30 days of our receipt of the request. Please note that we retain billing and usage metadata about a company or individual as required for compliance with law and regulation.Data Correction. You can modify your personal data by sending an email to hello@parasail.io with “Please update my personal data” in the subject line. Parasail will verify your ability to access that email, then update the fields where possible within the Services. We will use reasonable efforts to respond to your request within 30 days of our receipt of the request. Your Communications Preferences. You can choose whether you wish to receive marketing communications from us. If you receive marketing communications from us and would like to opt out, you can do so by following the directions in that communication. Alternatively, you can request that we withdraw consent to use your personal data by sending an email to hello@parasail.io with “Please withdraw my consent” in the subject line. Parasail will verify your ability to access that email, then delete the personal data associated with your email address. We will use reasonable efforts to respond to your request within 30 days of our receipt of the request. Please note that these choices do not apply to mandatory communications that are part of the Platform or Service.12. Children’s Personal DataThe Platform and the Service are not directed to, and we do not intend to, or knowingly, collect or solicit personal data from children under the age of 13. If an individual is under the age of 13, they should not use the Platform or the Service, or otherwise provide us with any personal data (either directly, or by any other means). If a child under the age of 13 has provided personal data to us, we encourage the child’s parent or guardian to contact us to request that we remove the personal data from our systems. If we learn that any personal data we have collected has been provided by a child under the age of 13, we will promptly delete that personal data.13. Third Party LinksOccasionally, at our discretion, we may include or offer third party products or services on our website or through the Platform or Service. If you access other websites using the links provided, the operators of these websites may collect information from you that will be used by those websites in accordance with their privacy policies. These third party sites have separate and independent privacy policies. We, therefore, have no responsibility or liability for the content and activities of these linked sites. 14. Changes to this PolicyWe will update this Policy as necessary to reflect customer feedback and changes in our Platform and Service. When we post changes to this Policy, we will revise the "last updated" date at the top of the Policy. If there are material changes to the Policy or in how Parasail will use your personal data, we will notify you either by prominently posting a notice of such changes before they take effect or by sending you a direct notification. We encourage you to periodically review this Policy to learn how Parasail is using your personal data.15. Contact UsIf you have any questions regarding this Policy, or our Platform or Service, please send an email to hello@parasail.ioReady to unlock the power of AI?Join other developers who are already using Parasail to optimize their workloads and cut costs.Get started with free credits today.First NameLast NameEmail AddressPhone NumberYour MessageThank you!Your submission has been received!Oops! websiteContent.mainContent: Terms of ServiceLast Updated: November 10, 2025Welcome to Parasail! We’re glad you’re here. These Terms of Service (these “Terms”) describe your rights and responsibilities when using our on-demand GPU inference services located at www.parasail.io, saas.parasail.io, and api.parasail.io (collectively, the “Platform”). Please read these Terms carefully; if you don’t agree to be bound by these Terms, you aren’t allowed to access or use the Platform. 1. Things You Should Know1.1 These Terms Form a Binding ContractUnless the parties have mutually executed a different agreement covering your use of the Platform, these Terms form a binding contract between Customer and Parasail. “We,” “our,” and “us” refers to Parasail, Inc., while “Customer” or “you” refers to you (or, as applicable, to the company or entity that you represent). The “Effective Date” of these Terms is the date that you first use our website or access any part of our Platform. If you access or use the Platform in your capacity as an employee, consultant or agent of a company or other entity, you represent that you are an employee, consultant or agent of that company or entity, and that you have the authority to bind that company or entity to these Terms. 1.2 These Terms Contain an Arbitration ProvisionThese Terms contain an arbitration provision (see Section 12, below). Please review the arbitration provision carefully, since it affects your rights. By using the Platform or any part of it, you understand and agree to be bound by the arbitration provision.1.3 There are Three Ways to Use the PlatformThere are currently three ways to use the Platform: “serverless”, “dedicated”, and “batch” (each, a “Service”). We provide details about each Service in Section 3 below. We may modify Services or add new Services from time to time. 1.4. Making Your Purchasing DecisionsWe may share information about our future product plans with you, because we want to get product feedback from you early and often. Our public statements about those product plans are only an expression of intent – please do not rely on them when making a purchase. If you decide to use our Platform, that decision should be based on the services, functionality and features we have available today, and not on the delivery of any services, functionality or features that may be available in the future.1.5 Beta ProductsOccasionally, we may beta test new features or functionality. These may be identified as “beta,” “pre-release,” or “early access,” or words or phrases with similar meanings (each, a “Beta Product”). Beta Products may not be ready for prime time, so we make them available “as is,” without warranty of any kind, express or implied. Should you encounter any faults with our Beta Products, we would love to hear about them; our primary reason for running beta programs is to iron out issues before making a new feature or function widely available.1.6 We Want Your FeedbackThe more suggestions our customers make, the better the Platform becomes. If you send us any feedback or suggestions regarding the Platform (whether via feedback.parasail.io, or any other way), there is a chance we will use them, so you grant us (on behalf of you, and the entity you represent) an unlimited, irrevocable, perpetual, sublicensable, transferable, royalty-free license to use any such feedback or suggestions for any purpose, without any obligation or compensation to you or your personnel. 1.7 We Can Modify These TermsWe reserve the right to change or modify these Terms, or any of our other policies or guidelines, at any time upon notice to you. We may provide that notice in a variety of ways, including, among other things, sending you an email, posting a notice on the Platform or Service itself, or by posting the revised Terms on our website and revising the date at the top of these Terms. Any changes or modifications will be effective after we provide notice that these Terms have been modified. You acknowledge that your continued use of the Platform following such notice constitutes your acceptance of the modified Terms.1.8 We Can Also Modify the PlatformWe reserve the right — at any time, and without notice or liability to you — to modify the Platform, or any part of it, temporarily or permanently. We may modify the Platform for a variety of reasons, including, among other things, adding new features or Services, implementing new protocols, maintaining compatibility with emerging standards, or complying with regulatory requirements.2. Accessing and Using the Platform 2.1 Access GrantSubject to your continued compliance with these Terms (including, among other things, paying the fees you owe us), Parasail grants you a limited, non-transferable, non-exclusive, revocable right and license to access and use the Platform, solely for your own internal business purposes. 2.2 RestrictionsExcept as expressly authorized by these Terms, you may not: (a) modify, disclose, alter, translate or create derivative works of the Platform; (b) license, sublicense, resell, distribute, lease, rent, lend, transfer, assign or otherwise dispose of the Platform (or any part of it); (c) offer any part of the Platform on a timeshare or service bureau basis; (d) allow or permit any third party to access or use the Platform; (e) use the Platform to store or transmit any viruses, software routines, or other code designed to permit anyone to access in an unauthorized manner, disable, erase or otherwise harm software, hardware, or data, or to perform any other harmful actions; (f) use the Platform to build a competitive product or service, or to copy any features or functions of the Platform (including, without limitation, the look-and-feel of the Platform); (g) interfere with or disrupt the integrity or performance of the Platform; (h) publicly disclose to any third party any performance information or analysis relating to the Platform; (i) remove, alter or obscure any proprietary notices in or on the Platform, including copyright notices; (j) use the Platform or any product thereof for any illegal or unauthorized purpose, or in a manner which violates any laws or regulations in your jurisdiction; (k) reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or otherwise attempt to discover the source code, object code, or underlying structure, ideas, or algorithms that make up the Platform or any software, documentation, or data relating to the Platform, except to the limited extent that applicable law prohibits such a restriction; (l) use any robot, spider, scraper, off-line reader, data mining tool, or any other automated means to access the Platform in a manner than sends more request messages to the servers running the Platform than a human can reasonably produce in the same period of time by using a conventional online web browser; (m) use any content available on or via the Platform (including any caption information, keywords, or other metadata) for any machine learning and/or artificial intelligence training or development purposes, or for any technologies designed or intended for the identification of natural persons; (n) buy, sell, or transfer API keys without our prior written consent in each case; (o) take any action that imposes, or may impose (as determined by us, in our sole discretion), an unreasonable or disproportionately large load on our infrastructure; or (p) cause or permit any third party to do any of the foregoing.2.3 Definitions. Here are some other definitions that we use when describing the Platform: (a) “Content” means, collectively, Customer Input and Output; (b) “Customer Input” means any data, images, code, or other content (including, without limitation, text, graphics, audio files, video files, or computer software) that you either: (i) publish, upload to, or use in conjunction with the Platform; (ii) make available in conjunction with the Platform, or (iii) allow the Platform to access; (c) “Model” means a pre-trained machine learning model, including algorithms and weights, which can be run to make predictions or queried to produce Output; and (d) “Output” means content and/or data generated by the Platform, and/or one or more Model(s), in response to a Customer query.2.4 You Are Responsible for Your ContentYou acknowledge and agree that you are entirely responsible for your Content, and for any harm or liability resulting from or arising out of that Content. Your responsibility applies whether or not you were the original creator or owner of the Content.By making Customer Input available to the Platform, you represent and warrant that: (a) the Customer Input does not and will not infringe, violate or misappropriate the intellectual property rights of any third party; (b) you have obtained all rights and permissions necessary to provide us with access to the Customer Input, and to use the Customer Input in the manner in which you have used it; (c) Parasail’s use of the Customer Input for the purpose of providing the Platform does not and will not (i) violate any applicable laws or regulations, or (ii) infringe, violate, or misappropriate the intellectual property rights of any third party; (d) you have fully complied with any third-party licenses relating to the Customer Input; (e) the Customer Input does not contain or install any viruses, worms, malware, Trojan horses or other harmful or destructive code; and (f) the Customer Input does not and will not include any: (i) “personal health information,” as defined under the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act, unless you have entered into a separate agreement with us relating to the processing of such data; (ii) government issued identification numbers, including Social Security numbers, drivers’ license numbers or other state-issued identification numbers; (iii) financial account information, including bank account numbers; (iv) payment card data, including credit card or debit card numbers; (v) personal information of children under 13 or the applicable age of digital consent; or (vi) any other sensitive personal data (including, without limitation, “sensitive data” as defined under Directive 95/46/EC of the European Parliament (the “EU Directive”) and any national laws adopted pursuant to the EU Directive, about residents of Switzerland and any member country of the European Union, including racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious beliefs, trade union membership, physical or mental health or condition, sexual life, or the commission or alleged commission any crime or offense).2.5 You Grant Us a License to Use Your Content to Provide the Platform to YouBy making Customer Input available to the Platform, you grant Parasail a worldwide, royalty-free, and non-exclusive license to reproduce, view, store, and use the Customer Input for sole the purpose of providing the Platform and its related Services to you (including, without limitation, to generate Output). Per Section 2.7 below, we will not use your Customer Input to train our own models or to improve the Platform.By generating Output, you grant Parasail a worldwide, royalty-free, and non-exclusive license to reproduce, view, store, and use the Output for the sole purpose of providing the Platform and related Services to you. Per Section 2.7 below, we will not use your Output to train our own models or to improve the Platform.2.6 If You Connect to the Platform via a Third-Party ServiceBy connecting to the Platform via any third-party service, you grant us permission to access and use your information from that service, as permitted by that service, and to store your login credentials and/or access tokens for that service.2.7 We Won’t Use Your Content to Train Models, or to Improve the PlatformWe mean it. We will not use your Content to train our own models or to improve the Platform. 2.8 We Don’t Own the Models – We Just Host ThemWe don’t own or control the Models that we make available to you as part of the Platform. You get to choose which Models you want to use as part of the Platform – we don’t make that choice for you. Before using a particular Model (particularly proprietary Models, as opposed to open-source Models), we or the owner of that Model might require you to agree to an additional set of terms and conditions (each, a “Model EULA”). You acknowledge that by using a Model that contains a Model EULA, you agree to abide by the terms of the Model EULA. You further acknowledge that, since we don’t own or control the Models, we are not and will not be liable for any issues that may arise as the result of your use of (or inability to use) any Models. If you have an issue with a Model, you should reach out to the entity that developed and/or released the Model.2.9 Third Party VendorsYou understand that we work with third-party vendors and hosting partners to provide the necessary hardware, software, networking, storage, and related technology required to provide the Platform. We do not control the acts or omissions of those third-party vendors or hosting partners. With that in mind, you agree that, except to the limited extent required by law, we are not and will not be liable or responsible for the acts or omissions of any third-party vendors or hosting partners we engage to help make the Platform available to you.2.10 RegistrationYou will be required to provide accurate, current and complete information about you as part of the registration process that enables you to access the Platform (“Registration Data”). As part of the registration process, you acknowledge and agree that we may require you and/or your end users to authenticate yourselves via a central authentication system, or via a single sign on system, in order to access the Platform. You agree: (a) to maintain the security of your password(s) that you use to access the Platform; (b) to maintain and promptly update your Registration Data (as well as any other information you provide to us) and to keep it accurate, current and complete; (c) to accept all risks of unauthorized access to your Registration Data, and any other information you provide to us, via your account(s) or password(s); (d) that you are responsible for maintaining the security of your account and safeguarding your password(s); and (e) that you will be fully responsible for any activities or transactions that take place using your account(s) or password(s). 2.11 You Will Need Your Own EquipmentYou understand that you will be solely responsible for obtaining and maintaining any equipment and ancillary services needed to connect to, access or otherwise use the Platform, including, without limitation, modems, hardware, servers, software, operating systems, networking, web servers and the like (collectively, “Equipment”). You will be solely responsible for maintaining the security of the Equipment, your accounts, passwords (including but not limited to administrative and user passwords) and files, and for all uses of your accounts, or the Equipment, that may occur, with or without your knowledge or consent. 3. Service Descriptions3.1 Serverless. Description: The “serverless” Service is our multitenant option, in which you can access a large pool of different Models via a Parasail API. We will provide you with a code (which you can upload into your own user interface, if you wish), and an API key. Take good care of them - you agree to be fully responsible for any activities or transactions that take place using your code or API key. Pricing: Pricing for the “serverless” Service is token-based (both input and output tokens), and the amount you owe will change depending on which Model(s) you choose to use.SLOs: We do not offer service level commitments of any kind for the “serverless” Service.Data Retention: We will not inspect your Content without your permission, and will only retain your Content for as long as is necessary to provide the Service to you (i.e., for the time it takes to generate Output and deliver that Output to you). You understand and agree that we may, from time to time, inspect prompt and usage metadata associated with your use of the Service (e.g., flags, parameters, and prompt/output sizes), provided that: (a) such inspection will only be for the purpose of providing the Service to you, or related support; and (b) the prompt and usage metadata will be masked. 3.2 Dedicated. Description: The “dedicated” Service is our single-tenant option, in which we reserve specific hardware for you. You choose which Model(s) get installed on your dedicated server, and only you are permitted to access that server. We reserve the right to swap out equipment as we deem appropriate from time to time, without notice or liability to you. We may also swap out the provider of the hardware. If we swap out equipment or providers, we will work to minimize any latency or downtime you experience, and to provide you with similar levels of performance. If, for compliance purposes or other reasons, you wish to require us to work within a particular provider’s cloud, you need to let us know that; you acknowledge that there may be an additional fee associated with this election.Pricing: Pricing for the “dedicated” Service is on a GPU hours basis.SLOs: We do offer certain service level objectives for the “dedicated” Service, as more fully set forth here. You acknowledge that the service level objectives are aspirational, and not a guarantee of uptime, response times, or performance. Data Retention: We will not inspect your Content without your permission, and will only retain your Content for as long as is necessary to provide the Service to you (i.e., for the time it takes to generate Output and deliver that Output to you). You understand and agree that we may, from time to time, inspect prompt and usage metadata associated with your use of the Service (e.g., flags, parameters, and prompt/output sizes), provided that: (a) such inspection will only be for the purpose of providing the Service to you, or related support; and (b) the prompt and usage metadata will be masked.3.3 Batch. Description: The “batch” Service is a way for you to upload large amounts of Customer Inputs, and then query one or more Models against that data. The Platform will help you index and query that data. You will receive the results of the query as Output, which you can then store on your own servers. In the “batch” Service, rather than using a multitenant or single-tenant model, your data will be stored directly on Parasail’s servers. The query process will not be run in real-time, which means that it may take some time (depending on the amount of Customer Inputs, and the Model(s) you choose to use) to receive the results of your queries.Pricing: Pricing for the “batch” Service is token-based (both input and output tokens, discounted to reflect the fact that your queries will not be processed in real time), and the amount you owe will change depending on which Model(s) you choose to use.SLOs: We do not offer service level commitments of any kind for the “batch” Service. We will run your queries behind the scenes, and Output will be provided to you as it becomes available.Data Retention: due to the nature of the “batch” Service, we will need to store any Customer Inputs you send us. As a result, to the extent you use the “batch” Service, you hereby grant us a non-exclusive, worldwide, limited license to the Customer Inputs for the purpose of providing the Service to you. As general rule, we will store your Customer Inputs for 30 days, though the specific timeframe may vary. We will also store your Output – the answers to your queries – on our servers, and, to the extent you use the “batch” Service, you hereby you grant us a non-exclusive, worldwide, limited license to the Output for the purpose of providing the Service to you. To be clear, the foregoing license to Output is only to enable us to store the Output for the purpose of providing the Service to you – we won’t access or use the Output for any other purpose. As general rule, we store your Output for 30 days, though specific timeframes may vary. 4. Privacy PolicyPlease review our Privacy Policy at https://www.parasail.io/legal/privacy-policy for more information on how we collect and use data relating to the use and performance of our Platform. By using the Platform, you consent to our collection, use, and disclosure of information as set forth in our Privacy Policy, as we may update that Policy from time to time.5. Payment Obligations5.1 Payment TermsFees due for your use of the Platform are payable monthly in arrears, unless otherwise agreed between the parties in writing. You will need to provide us with your credit card details in order to access and use the Platform. You agree that we can automatically charge your credit card for all amounts owed each month. By providing us (or our designated payment platform processing provider) with your credit card information, you agree: (a) that you have the right to provide that information to us; (b) that we are authorized to charge that card for all fees due to us hereunder, and that no additional notice or consent is required; and (c) that you will keep your credit card information up to date. If we agree to invoice you, full payment must be received within thirty (30) days of the invoice date. Payment obligations are non-cancelable and, except as expressly stated in these Terms, fees paid are non-refundable. Fees are stated exclusive of any taxes, levies, duties, or similar governmental assessments of any nature, including, for example, value-added, sales, use or withholding taxes, assessable by any jurisdiction (collectively, “Taxes”). You will be responsible for paying all Taxes associated with your purchases, except for those taxes based on our net income. Should any payment for the Platform be subject to withholding tax by any government, you will reimburse us for such withholding tax. You agree to make all payments in U.S. Dollars, unless the Order Form indicates otherwise. 5.2 Interest on Late Payments Interest on any late payments will accrue at the rate of 1.5% per month, or the highest rate permitted by law, whichever is lower, from the date the amount is due until the date the amount is paid in full. If you are late in paying us, you also agree that, in addition to our rights to suspend your access to the Platform, terminate your account(s), and/or pursue any other rights or remedies available to us at law or in equity, you are responsible to reimburse us for any costs that we incur while attempting to collect such late payments. 6. Ownership and Proprietary Rights6.1 The Things You Own, and the Things We OwnAs between us, you own all of your Content, and all related intellectual property rights. We own and will continue to own the Platform, including all related intellectual property rights. We may make software components or APIs available as part of the Platform; to the extent we do so, we grant you a non-sublicensable, non-transferable, non-exclusive, limited license to use the object code version of these components, solely as necessary to use the Platform in accordance with these Terms. All of our rights not expressly granted by this license are hereby retained.6.2 Usage DataYou acknowledge and agree that we may gather and utilize aggregated information concerning your use of the Platform for the purpose of monitoring and improving the Platform generally, provided that (a) such information does not include your Content; and (b) such information does not identify you, your customers, or your users.6.3 Aggregated or Anonymized DataYou acknowledge that we have the right to collect, use and share data that has been aggregated or anonymized (so that it does not directly or indirectly identify you, your customers, or your users) for the purpose of developing new services and features, as well as promoting our products and services through, for example, publishing analyses of general patterns and trends. 7. researchContext.claimedTam: $1.5T Cloud Compute Market By 2032; Inference market projected to reach $365B by 2032
researchContext.businessModel: Usage-based inference ("API based on Token Usage" for Serverless); pay-as-you-go transparent pricing with "no contracts or commitments" and billing only when dedicated models are active (pause/resume); dedicated private endpoints priced by GPU/hour ("lowest hourly rate GPUs anywhere") with scaling via load-balanced replicas; batch processing for "thousands or millions" of inferences (OpenAI Batch compatible); enterprise sales cycle indicated ("10 day average to sign") plus LOIs (e.g., "$5M LOI")
researchContext.claimedGrowth: ~19.2% Inference CAGR; "Rapid growth in 5 months public"
researchContext.specificMarket: Demand-side AI inference platform + supply-side AI Deployment Network for low-cost, high-performance LLM inference (tokens/API) across a unified global GPU network ("Planetary Kubernetes") spanning neo clouds, alternative AI HW clouds, and tier-2 datacenters
researchContext.geographicFocus: Global / worldwide ("Run workloads anywhere in the world"; "unified global GPU network"; GPUs hosted in "15+ countries"; "25+" global clouds / compute providers including US and EU regions referenced)
researchContext.targetCustomers: AI developers and the fastest growing AI companies consuming compute as tokens; production inference teams at startups and enterprises building LLM apps; enterprise customers needing private "Dedicated Endpoints" for production QoS and security/compliance; builders of real-time "Voice Agents" (STT → LLM → TTS pipelines) and "Image & Video Understanding" workloads; customers migrating inference traffic from providers like Groq; organizations wanting Parasail to "manage 42 racks of hardware" (LOI-driven infrastructure management / deployment network use case)
researchContext.productDescription: Parasail is an AI inference platform that aggregates and orchestrates the Earth’s GPU resources through an "AI Deployment Network" and "Planetary Kubernetes" control plane, routing customer "units of AI work" (token-based API inference, batch inferencing, and dedicated private endpoints) across 25+ compute providers and 15+ countries. It provides serverless models via an OpenAI-spec compatible API gateway (https://api.parasail.io/v1), batch processing (OpenAI Batch API compatible), and dedicated endpoints that can run most HuggingFace transformers on selected GPU types (e.g., 4090s up to H200s) with features like load-balanced replicas, performance/QoS control, and optimization techniques (quantization, speculative decoding), positioning as "no limits, no contracts" and "up to 30× cheaper than legacy cloud."

web_research_data.formattedForPrompt:
=== COMPANY WEBSITE CONTENT ===

copyCopychevron-downDedicated InstanceDedicated EndpointsDedicated endpoints are private endpoints that you can in a few clicks run most transformers on HuggingFace on our full range of GPU offerings at the best prices on the market. Dedicated endpoints are ideal for creating production endpoints with full control over performance and QOS, and for experimenting with models that are not available on public serverless endpoints.hashtagAdvantages of Dedicated Endpoints:Great for both experimenting and deploying to production: run most transformers on HuggingFace by simply entering the URL.Run custom models hosted in your private HuggingFace repo by simply entering an access token.Select from a variety of hardware types—4090 s up to H200 s—to match your cost and performance needs.Scale your instance up as traffic grows with load balanced replicas.Hit aggressive latency targets through methods like GPU selection, model quantization, and speculative decoding.Great Customer Value: Our dedicated model service delivers great value to customers through easy on-demand usage for even large scale deployments, no contracts or commitments, and the lowest hourly rate GPUs anywhere. You can pause and resume models at any time and billing is only activated when the model is active.Model Compatibility: Behind the scenes, we work with a variety of inference stacks to maintain the best model compatibility and performance. Depending on how a model is launched, if it is coordinated with the community (for example Llama models), we will have 0-day support. Sometimes if models are dropped unannounced it can take a few days to a week to get support in. If a model is released and we do not offer support please let us know on our Discord [LINK] or through our support channels and we will investigate.We currently support most transformers, while support for diffusion models and more conventional AI models like CNNs or LSTMs are further down our roadmap. Some models are also not supported because they contain local code execution in the model repo, however we are able to whitelist trusted model providers so please contact us if you would like us to investigate a model support issue.hashtagCreating a Dedicated EndpointAfter clicking on the Dedicated Tab, the configuration page will be shown:Deployment Name:Guide me (deprecated): this is a feature being deprecated that allowed for a click-through selection of popular AI models.HuggingFace ID / URL: this is where you specify the model to load. Currently we only support models hosted either publicly or privately on HuggingFace. If your model is local, its very easy to load it into a private HuggingFace repo. For public models, you can specify either the URL or the model ID.For example, for the FP8 version of Qwen 2 72 B Instruct, you can specify:https://huggingface.co/neuralmagic/Qwen2-72B-Instruct-FP8arrow-up-right or neuralmagic/Qwen2-72B-Instruct-FP8FYI HuggingFace has a convenient copy button next to the model ID:HuggingFace

copyCopychevron-downBatchQuick startThis page gives an introduction to Parasail's Batch Processing Library. For more detailed information, jump to:Using the Batch UIParasail's Python Batch Helper Library ReferenceBatch file format (100% compatible with OpenAI)OpenAI Batch API reference (Parasail is 100% compatible)Parasail's batch processing engine is a straightforward and inexpensive way to process thousands or millions of LLM inferences. Batch inferencing is easy: create an input file, start a batch job, wait for it to finish, then download the output.OpenAI compatibility, Parasail batch helper libraryhashtagGetting Started with the Parasail Batch Helper Librarycircle-infoYou can find the Parasail Batch Helper Library on Github. This library is 100% compatible with both Parasail and OpenAI https://github.com/parasail-ai/openai-batcharrow-up-rightThe first step is to create a Parasail API key: https://www.saas.parasail.io/keysarrow-up-right. This key should be stored in your environment through something like a .bashrc file or a .env file, or passed through the command line invocation. Pasting directly in code isn't recommended as keys can get leaked.Next, install openai-batch, the batch helper library that's compatible both with Parasail and OpenAI:Copypip install openai-batchNow you're ready to run a batch job in as little as five lines of code. The batch endpoint supports most transformers on Hugging Face—all you have to do is put the Hugging Face ID in the request. There's no need for this model to be a dedicated or endpoint. In this example, Parasail uses NousResearch/DeepHermes-3-Mistral-24 B-Preview (https://huggingface.co/NousResearch/DeepHermes-3-Mistral-24B-Previewarrow-up-right).Copy#test_batch.py import random from openai_batch import Batch # Create a batch with random prompts with Batch() as batch: objects = ["cat", "robot", "coffee mug", "spaceship", "banana"] for i in range(100): batch.add_to_batch( model="NousResearch/DeepHermes-3-Mistral-24B-Preview", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"Tell me a joke about a {random.choice(objects)}"}] ) #temporary workaround file_path = batch.submission_input_file.name batch.submission_input_file.close() batch.submission_input_file = file_path batch._should_close = False # Submit, wait for completion, and download results result, output_path, error_path = batch.submit_wait_download() print(f"Batch completed with status {result.status} and stored in {output_path}")This code looks for PARASAIL_API_KEY in the environment, and passing it through the command line is straightforward. === MARKET RESEARCH ===

TAM: $113 billion
Growth Rate: 17.5%
Trends: Shift in AI economics from training-heavy to inference-heavy spend as more workloads move into production (LLM apps, agents, real-time pipelines).; Multi-cloud / cross-cloud frameworks and distributed cloud adoption increase demand for a “unified control plane” across heterogeneous GPU fleets (neo-clouds, alt-HW clouds, colo/tier-2 datacenters).; “Dedicated endpoints” and private inference deployments grow due to sovereignty, privacy, and predictable QoS requirements (drives higher willingness-to-pay for isolation, locality, SLAs).; Hardware diversification: GPUs remain dominant, but inference-optimized silicon (ASIC/TPU/LPU/NPU) expands addressable supply and increases scheduling/portability complexity—favoring abstraction layers.; Latency-sensitive pipelines (voice agents: STT→LLM→TTS; video understanding) drive “inference at the edge/near-edge” and regional POP buildouts; routing and locality become product differentiators.; FinOps for AI emerges: customers increasingly demand token-level unit economics, admission control, caching, batching, quantization, and dynamic routing to minimize $/task while meeting SLOs.
Drivers: Explosive growth in public cloud and AI workloads: Gartner forecasts public cloud services reaching $1.42T by 2029, with AI workloads a key driver. ([gartner.com](https://www.gartner.com/en/documents/6302015?utm_source=openai)), Rapid expansion of AI infrastructure investment: Gartner forecasts total AI spending of $2.52T in 2026 and $3.34T in 2027, with AI infrastructure the largest component. ([gartner.com](https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2026-1-15-gartner-says-worldwide-ai-spending-will-total-2-point-5-trillion-dollars-in-2026?utm_source=openai)), Accelerated server buildout for AI: IDC projects AI infrastructure spending to reach $107B by 2028, with cloud deployments comprising the majority. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20241126982316/en/Artificial-Intelligence-Infrastructure-Spending-to-Surpass-the-%24100B-USD-Mark-in-the-Next-5-years-According-to-IDC?utm_source=openai)), AI inference market growth (multiple third-party estimates) indicating a large and expanding spend pool for inference hardware + software + platforms (e.g., $253.75B by 2030). ([grandviewresearch.com](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/artificial-intelligence-ai-inference-market-report?utm_source=openai)), Developers and fast-growing AI-native companies increasingly consume compute as an API (tokens), creating demand for elastic capacity, global routing, and predictable performance.
Challenges: Supply-side volatility and constraints: GPU availability, HBM/memory bottlenecks, and power/cooling limits can cap capacity and raise costs for inference providers., Hyperscaler competition and bundling: AWS/Azure/GCP can bundle AI infrastructure + managed services, compressing margins for independent inference platforms., Performance variability across heterogeneous infrastructure (different GPUs/ASICs, networks, datacenter quality) makes consistent QoS/SLA delivery and debugging harder—especially for real-time voice/video pipelines., Unit economics pressure: token prices are falling in some segments; providers must offset with higher utilization (batching, caching, quantization), better routing, and superior procurement/supply contracts., Security and compliance overhead for enterprise “dedicated endpoints” (SOC2/ISO, encryption, key management, logging, tenant isolation) increases time-to-revenue and operational cost.

=== PRODUCT & COMPETITIVE RESEARCH ===

Product: Parasail is an AI inference platform and “AI Deployment Network” that aggregates GPU capacity across a large set of third-party compute providers and routes customer inference workloads over a unified control plane it markets as “Planetary Kubernetes.” It supports (1) serverless, token-priced model APIs via an OpenAI-spec compatible gateway (base URL https://api.parasail.io/v1), (2) OpenAI Batch API-compatible asynchronous batch processing for large volumes of requests, and (3) dedicated private endpoints to deploy most Hugging Face transformers on selected GPU types (from RTX 4090-class up through H200-class), with features including load-balanced replicas, pause/resume billing, and performance optimization techniques such as quantization and speculative decoding. Parasail positions itself as “no limits, no contracts,” aiming to undercut hyperscaler economics by sourcing capacity globally across 15+ countries and 25+ clouds while giving developers near-drop-in migration from OpenAI-compatible clients.
Strengths: OpenAI-compatible developer experience (drop-in base_url swap) reduces switching friction from OpenAI-style clients and tooling, Network/aggregation positioning: can source capacity across many providers and geographies (claim of 25+ clouds and 15+ countries) to reduce GPU scarcity risk and improve cost/latency options, Multiple consumption modes match common inference patterns: serverless tokens, batch async, and reserved dedicated endpoints, Dedicated endpoints include practical production controls (replicas/load balancing, pause/resume billing, QoS controls like max concurrent connections per replica), Clear platform stance against long-term commitments (“no contracts”), aligning with buyer desire to avoid hyperscaler lock-in
Weaknesses: Limited third-party software marketplace review coverage (G2 shows 0 reviews; Capterra/TrustRadius listings not found; Product Hunt review visibility inconsistent), Operational complexity/risk inherent to multi-provider orchestration: performance, availability, and data residency depend on upstream providers and routing decisions, Docs indicate some model support may lag for unannounced releases; some models are unsupported due to local code execution in repos (requires whitelisting/trust process), Rate limits and GPU quotas exist in practice (e.g., RPM tiers; initial per-org GPU quota), which can conflict with “no limits” marketing for some users, Dedicated endpoint management API recommends using UI first due to limited visibility into GPU performance recommendations and HF compatibility issues
Position: Parasail is positioned as a hybrid: a demand-side inference API (OpenAI-compatible, token-based) plus a supply-side “deployment network” that arbitrages global GPU supply across providers. Versus hyperscalers, Parasail competes on price, faster access to frontier/open models, and reduced procurement friction (no commitments). Versus single-provider neoclouds (CoreWeave, Lambda, Nebius), Parasail’s differentiation is orchestration/aggregation across many providers and offering an opinionated inference product surface (serverless + OpenAI Batch compatibility + managed dedicated endpoints) rather than primarily selling raw GPU instances. The key strategic bet is that routing and pooling fragmented GPU supply can deliver sustainably lower costs and better availability than any single cloud while keeping integration friction minimal through OpenAI compatibility.

Competitors:
- Together AI: AI cloud platform offering serverless inference APIs for open models, fine-tuning, and batch inference; also offers GPU cluster products for larger-scale needs. (Funding: $411M+ publicly disclosed (includes $106M round in March 2024 and $305M Series B in Feb 2025))
- Fireworks AI: Inference platform offering serverless model APIs, fine-tuning, and on-demand GPU deployments; positioned for enterprise production workloads and compound AI systems. (Funding: $327M+ (per Series C announcement; includes prior rounds))
- Baseten: Inference platform for AI-native products focused on optimized runtimes, autoscaling, and multi-cloud capacity management; supports model APIs and dedicated deployments. (Funding: $285M+ (per Series D announcement))
- Modal: Serverless compute platform (GPU/CPU) for running AI workloads (inference, batch, pipelines) with per-second billing; positioned as infrastructure for AI apps rather than only model APIs. (Funding: $111M (per company Series B post))
- Replicate: Platform for running and deploying ML models via API; strong marketplace/community model catalog; billing commonly per-second by underlying hardware with some token-based models. (Funding: $58M (per Forbes report; Series B in Dec 2023))
- Groq (GroqCloud): Alternative AI hardware (LPU) and hosted inference service (GroqCloud) marketed for very fast token generation; offers token-based API pricing for select models. (Funding: $1.39B+ publicly disclosed (includes $640M round in Aug 2024 per Reuters and $750M financing Sep 2025))
- Cerebras (Cerebras Inference): AI hardware company offering high-speed inference (and training systems) including a hosted inference product; positions on speed and price-performance vs GPU-based inference. (Funding: $1.1B in Series G (Sep 30, 2025); total lifetime funding varies by source)
- CoreWeave: GPU-focused cloud provider (“neocloud”) offering GPU instances/clusters and managed services; competes as a capacity provider for training and inference backends. (Funding: Public company (IPO Mar 2025 raised ~$1.5B) plus subsequent strategic investments (e.g., NVIDIA $2B stock investment announced Jan 2026))
- Lambda (Lambda GPU Cloud): GPU cloud provider offering on-demand instances, clusters, and private cloud for large-scale AI workloads; competes on GPU availability and pricing. (Funding: $863M total equity capital raised (per Series D announcement))
- Nebius AI Cloud / Nebius Group: GPU-focused AI cloud (“neocloud”) offering on-demand and committed GPU instances/clusters (H100/H200/B200/GB200) with emphasis on AI workloads. (Funding: $700M raised from investors including NVIDIA and Accel (reported after Nasdaq relisting; Nebius is public))
- RunPod: GPU cloud with serverless and on-demand offerings; provides per-second serverless pricing and supports running inference workers. (Funding: Not reliably found in publicly accessible primary sources during this research pass)
- AWS (Bedrock / SageMaker / EC2 GPU): Hyperscaler cloud offering managed foundation model access (Bedrock), model deployment tooling (SageMaker), and GPU infrastructure (EC2) for inference/training. (Funding: Public company (NASDAQ: AMZN); hyperscaler business funded via corporate cashflows and public markets)
- Microsoft Azure (Azure OpenAI and AI services): Hyperscaler cloud offering managed model APIs and enterprise AI services; competes with integrated enterprise platform and strong OpenAI partnership distribution. (Funding: Public company (NASDAQ: MSFT); funded via corporate cashflows and public markets)
- Google Cloud (Vertex AI): Hyperscaler cloud offering Vertex AI for model hosting, MLOps, and managed foundation model access; competes on integrated data/AI platform and TPU/GPU options. (Funding: Public company (NASDAQ: GOOGL); funded via corporate cashflows and public markets)
- Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI): Hyperscaler/major cloud offering GPU instances and managed AI services; competes on enterprise relationships and aggressive capacity investments. (Funding: Public company (NYSE: ORCL); funded via corporate cashflows and public markets)

startup_evaluations.executive_summary:
Parasail is a Series A-stage cloud infrastructure company building an “AI Deployment Network” for inference: a single, OpenAI-compatible platform that routes workloads across a federated pool of third-party GPU providers (25+ providers across 15+ countries). The core opportunity is to become the control plane for production inference—abstracting away quota constraints, multi-region ops, and heterogeneous hardware—while delivering materially better unit economics (the deck claims up to 30× cheaper) for AI-native teams. Our thesis is that as inference becomes the dominant run-rate compute spend and GPU supply continues to fragment across neo-clouds and datacenters, the winning platform may be the one that can reliably orchestrate and optimize across that fragmentation rather than owning the GPUs outright.

The team is anchored by CEO/founder Mike Henry, a repeat AI compute operator with unusually direct founder–market fit (Mythic founder/CEO; interim CPO at Groq; PhD with relevant patents). He has credibility with both technical buyers and supply-side partners, and prior experience raising and scaling deep-tech organizations. Tim Harris (Swift Navigation CEO) adds strong BD/contracting and governance capability, which is valuable for a partnership-heavy supply network. The primary execution concern is organizational: there is no clearly evidenced, full-time CTO/VP Engineering and SRE leader with hyperscaler-grade distributed systems and reliability experience—an acute gap for a multi-tenant, multi-provider inference network where tail latency, incident response, and SLA discipline are existential.

Market timing is favorable. Independent estimates support a very large and fast-growing inference spend pool (high-teens CAGR), and the near-term “AI-optimized IaaS” segment is expanding rapidly as enterprises operationalize GenAI. Parasail’s traction, if validated, is standout for this stage: public launch in April 2025, $3M ARR in ~5 months, “$5M today” run-rate, 25–30 enterprise customers, and 75–80B+ tokens/day. The speed of adoption (claimed ~10 days to sign and rapid production cutovers) suggests strong painkiller value around cost/rate limits and time-to-production. Diligence should focus on revenue definitions (booked vs run-rate), cohort-based retention/expansion, and concentration given consumption dynamics.

The business model is primarily usage-based inference (tokens / workload execution), with packaging that spans serverless APIs, batch, and dedicated endpoints. The optimistic case is compelling: software-like leverage without GPU capex, with margin expansion driven by routing, dynamic batching, and KV-cache sharing. However, defensibility is currently moderate. The architecture (multi-provider orchestration + inference gateway) is replicable by well-capitalized inference platforms and, indirectly, hyperscalers. Parasail’s moat must compound through provider breadth, telemetry-driven optimization, and deep production embedding (workflow primitives, governance, observability). The biggest technical and commercial risk is delivering consistent QoS and compliance across heterogeneous third-party providers.

The proposed financing is a $20M Series A structured as a convertible note at a $130M pre-money reference valuation, with no lead secured. On valuation, this is aggressive on $3M ARR (~43×) but becomes more reasonable on an $8M 2025E run-rate (~16×) if that is highly visible and gross margin durability holds. The note structure at this size introduces cap-table complexity and increases the chance of investor-favorable mechanics without a lead setting market terms. Use of funds—scaling engineering and building the first GTM function—is directionally right, but operational hires (CTO/SRE, enterprise security/compliance) should be treated as critical-path rather than optional.

Recommendation: Proceed to deeper diligence with a bias toward a structured investment if (and only if) we can validate (1) contribution margin by cohort/workload, (2) retention and concentration, and (3) reliability/SLA performance across providers. Parasail has rare early usage/revenue velocity and a credible “control plane for inference” narrative with meaningful strategic exit pathways (chip vendors, enterprise control-plane players, possibly hyperscalers). Key risks are operational maturity (SRE/compliance), margin sustainability in a deflationary token market, and competitive bundling from hyperscalers and better-capitalized inference platforms. A conditional “invest/lead if we can syndicate and tighten terms” is appropriate; absent a lead and with unresolved operational gaps, the risk-adjusted posture is to invest only with governance/term protections and hiring commitments.

extracted_features.description: Unified platform for running production LLM inference with optimization across geography, price, latency, performance, and hardware selection.

extracted_features.name: AI Deployment Network
extracted_features.description: Supply-side network aggregating many compute providers to present on-demand GPU capacity as a single fleet.

extracted_features.name: Planetary Kubernetes
extracted_features.description: Single control plane to orchestrate GPU resources globally across multiple providers/regions, including heterogeneous environments.

extracted_features.name: Planetary scheduler and autoscaler
extracted_features.description: Global scheduling and autoscaling across providers/regions for inference workloads.

extracted_features.name: Routing and workload distribution
extracted_features.description: Traffic routing and distribution of inference workloads across the network to optimize latency/cost/performance.

extracted_features.name: Multicloud network virtualization
extracted_features.description: Networking layer to make multicloud deployment 'invisible' to customers and enable consistent connectivity/operations.

extracted_features.name: Security and governance
extracted_features.description: Unified DevOps/security operations across datacenters and providers; security/compliance considerations in routing.

extracted_features.name: Logging and observability
extracted_features.description: Centralized logging/observability across the deployment network.

extracted_features.name: L2 KV cache + KV cache sharing
extracted_features.description: Inference cache layer to improve throughput/latency and efficiency across inference pools.

extracted_features.name: Multi-engine inference
extracted_features.description: Automatically selects the best inference engine per model/application; 'invisible to customer'.

extracted_features.name: Model optimization
extracted_features.description: Model selection/tuning to application; optional kernel tuning case-by-case; automation to search design space.

extracted_features.name: Dataflow processing and dynamic batching
extracted_features.description: Batching/utilization strategies to reduce GPU idle time and improve fleet utilization; weave batch jobs into downtime.

extracted_features.name: Agentic workflows support
extracted_features.description: Support for research/search workflows, stateless agent deployment, and browser control workloads.

extracted_features.name: Complex modalities pipelines
extracted_features.description: Web search/scraping, real-time voice pipeline (via proprietary model partnerships), and RAG pipelines for generative media.

extracted_tech_stack.technology: KV cache (L2 KV Cache, KV Cache Sharing)

teamResearch.name: Mike Henry

patents.title: Systems and methods for mapping matrix calculations to a matrix multiply accelerator
Systems and methods for mapping matrix calculations to a matrix multiply accelerator
Systems and methods for mapping matrix calculations to a matrix multiply accelerator

educationHighlights: BS and PhD in Computer Engineering (Virginia Tech) reported by O'Reilly conference speaker bio.
notableAchievements: Conference speaker bio (2018) lists Mike Henry as Mythic CEO and states Mythic had raised $56M at that time (point-in-time claim).
TechCrunch reported Mythic underwent major restructuring; CEO role transitioned from Henry to CTO/other leadership after Henry departed.
pastAccomplishments: Co-founded and served as CEO of Mythic (AI inference/edge AI hardware company), originally operating under the name Isocline; Mythic described its origins as stemming from University of Michigan research, and Mike Henry was identified as co-founder/CEO in company communications.
Led Mythic through multiple venture financings; TechCrunch reported Mythic raised over $170M across rounds (Parasail’s own site states $165M raised).
Co-founded Parasail and serves as CEO (as listed on Parasail’s official site).

teamResearch.name: Timothy (Tim) Harris
teamResearch.role: Co-Founder, Board Member/Director (Parasail); Co-Founder & CEO (Swift Navigation)
notableAchievements: Swift Navigation announced recognition in Fast Company’s 'Next Big Things in Tech' list (Honorable Mention, 2021).
pastAccomplishments: Co-founded Swift Navigation and serves as CEO (per Swift leadership page and Parasail team page).
Swift Navigation has publicly discussed raising total capital to over $250M (as of its Series E announcement referenced in Swift’s Q&A post).
Co-founded Parasail and serves on the board (per Parasail’s official site).

teamMembers.bio: Over a decade of AI product leadership as founder and CEO of Mythic, a top VC-backed AI company and pioneer of delivering AI to the edge in a cost-efficient HW and SW platform. We invented new compute technology to unlock powerful capabilities in customer's products and set off an industry-wide movement. My mission is to continue my growth as a leader in the AI space and leverage my strong product mindset to serve customers facing the massive opportunities and perilous pitfalls of AI disruption.
