linkedinData.summary: Over a decade of AI product leadership as founder and CEO of Mythic, a top VC-backed AI company and pioneer of delivering AI to the edge in a cost-efficient HW and SW platform. We invented new compute technology to unlock powerful capabilities in customer's products and set off an industry-wide movement. My mission is to continue my growth as a leader in the AI space and leverage my strong product mindset to serve customers facing the massive opportunities and perilous pitfalls of AI disruption.
linkedinData.headline: CEO of Parasail - Bringing AI to everyone, everywhere
education: Virginia Tech - Ph.D, Electrical and Computer Engineering
Virginia Tech - M.S., Electrical and Computer Engineering
Virginia Tech - B.S., Electrical and Computer Engineering

experienceDetails.description:
I founded Parasail to rethink the AI cloud and help AI-native companies move as fast as possible

Legacy cloud providers weren’t built for AI. They rely on rigid contracts, limited availability, and massive hidden costs. Parasail is changing that.

We’ve built the first AI Deployment Network—giving teams access to the largest on-demand GPU network in the world. No quotas, no contracts, no complexity. Just the freedom to run and scale any model, anywhere, with full control over cost and performance.

We are building an open compute economy to power the AI revolution. It’s essential that AI doesn’t become a walled garden controlled by a few dominant players. The future of AI must be an open playing field—top to bottom.

experienceDetails.location: Mountain View, California, United States · On-site

experienceDetails.description:
Mythic is the pioneer of power- and cost-efficient AI platform, enabled by breakthrough analog compute technology. Mythic raised $165M and delivered the world’s first production-ready analog AI hardware and software stack to customers, setting off an industry-wide compute-in-memory movement. This role showcases a proven track record of identifying emerging technology trends, taking high-reward risks on new approaches, building a strong team, and leading product execution with relentless determination. 

Mythic: Venture-Funded Years  (2017-2023)
	- Organically built a world-class leadership team and staff of > 150 engineers across a unique range of technical disciplines to successfully transition analog compute to mass production.  
	- Raised $165M from top investors including BlackRock, Softbank, and DFJ. Secured corporate investors and strategic partners, including Micron, Lockheed Martin, IQT, DoD, and Cirrus Logic.
	- Led product and technology development, directly overseeing 4 VP-level leaders; navigated massive R&D and manufacturing challenges, while relentlessly focusing on velocity and accountability. 
	- Worked closely with the business team to build go-to-market strategy, focusing on high-end computer vision market.  Built $300M world-wide sales funnel.
	- Successfully established industry thought leadership and brand identity. Successes include two YouTube videos with > 9M views, articles in top industry news, and analog compute prominently featured in industry roadmaps.
	- Transitioned company to CTO in 2023 to focus on development of next-gen IP. 

Mythic: Government-Funded Years    (2012-2017)
	- Started Mythic after graduate school by winning a $150k DoD research grant, later expanding through relentless evangelizing within the DoD to $2.5M in grants to fund early R&D of Mythic’s core technology. Built core R&D team of 5 and proved the concepts of analog compute. Was technical and hands-on with chip, software, and AI algorithms during this time.

teamMembers.bio:
As founder and CEO of Swift Navigation, I am dedicated to helping Swift achieve its mission to enable a future of autonomous vehicles to navigate and understand the world. 

Our precision localization platform improves positioning accuracy from 4 meters to 4 centimeters, supporting a range of autonomous and effective navigation solutions. We are helping over 7,000 customers build the next generation of vehicles and devices. 

My role at Swift is simple; I hire the best people, point them in the right direction, and support them in their achievements. Be a part of our team at https://www.swiftnav.com/join-us

Run a company that is redefining localization and lead a skilled and talented team building precise positioning solutions that benefit customers across automotive, mass-market, commercial transport and mobile applications. 

We're hiring! www.swiftnav.com

At Parasail, we’re building the first AI Deployment Network—giving AI-native teams access to the largest on-demand GPU compute pool available, without contracts, quotas, or DevOps complexity.

Our platform matches workloads across a global network of high-performance GPUs (H100s, H200s, 4090s, and more), so companies can deploy custom or open-source models and run inference at up to 30x lower cost than traditional providers.

I joined Parasail to help AI builders regain control of their infrastructure and accelerate what’s possible—from real-time apps to massive batch processing and everything in between.

websiteContent.description: The fastest, most cost-efficient AI deployment network-no quotas, no long-term contracts, and up to 30x cheaper than legacy cloud providers.
websiteContent.mainContent: No limits.No contracts.Priced right.The world’s fastest, most cost-efficient AI inference network. No quotas, no lock-ins, and up to 30× cheaper than legacy cloud.Get started with free creditsRun any model on hugging faceScale from 0 to 10B+ tokens in hoursNo rate limits, no quotas30xCheaper than legacy clouds15+Countries hosting GPUsDay 0Support for frontier LLMs80B+Tokens served dailyOrchestrating theEarth’s GPU resourcesScaleGo from prototype to planetary scale in minutes, with no long-term commitmentsReachRun workloads anywhere in the world through our unified global GPU networkDiversityPick the right hardware for your needs, from top performance to low-cost efficiencyOne platform for every way AIsees, speaks, and thinks.Image & Video UnderstandingTransform raw visuals into instant intelligence through distributed inference built for scale and precision.Image & Video UnderstandingReal-time visual intelligence at scale: Turn images and videos into actionable insights like object detection, activity recognition, and scene understanding — fast enough for live analytics, moderation, or monitoring.Serverless AI pipelines for vision: Deploy, scale, and tune vision workflows declaratively. From frame decoding to multimodal embeddings, Parasail’s planetary infrastructure automatically optimizes for cost, latency, and geography.Flexible, model-agnostic performance: Run any vision or multimodal model; Parasail handles orchestration, routing, and caching across 25+ global clouds for peak performance and transparency.Voice AgentsRun natural, real-time voice experiences on colocated models engineered for ultra-low latency and cost.Voice AgentsConversational AI that feels human: Enable emotionally rich, real-time dialogue for assistants, companions, and agents with consistent sub-500 ms latency and expressive control over tone, emotion, and voice.Custom voice pipelines, not APIs: Combine best-in-class models like Whisper, Resemble, and DeepSeek into a unified STT → LLM → TTS stack built for streaming, voice cloning, and multilingual interaction.Optimized for every use case: Support customer service systems that respond instantly, creative tools that speak with personality, and interactive companions that think and talk naturally at a fraction of the cost of legacy APIs.Search & AgentsDeploy agentic systems on a global inference network that manages routing, caching, and orchestration to stay fast, reliable, and affordable.Search & AgentsAutonomous reasoning at scale: Power intelligent chat and research agents that can plan, search, and synthesize across billions of calls with full observability and control.Composable, inference-aware orchestration: Build complex multi-model chains (retrieval, synthesis, browser control, reflection) that scale seamlessly across Parasail’s global GPU network.Open models, transparent economics: Use the latest open-weight LLMs like DeepSeek, Qwen, or Llama for results that match proprietary APIs at a fraction of the cost.Text LLMsScale language workflows that deliver fast, factual, and reproducible results at production scale.‍Text LLMsLong-context, grounded generation: Combine streaming retrieval and memory with verification so long documents, pipelines, and multi-step synthesis stay accurate and auditable.Evaluation and iteration as part of the pipeline: Run large LLM evaluations, instruction tuning, and synthetic data generation with versioned models, reproducible prompts, and metrics baked into CI.Inference-as-code and model control: Declare tokenization, retrieval, prompting, and fine-tune steps as code so experiments move to production with identical behavior and transparent economics.Deploy your way.ServerlessRun any model instantly with pay-per-token pricing and zero setup — ideal for quick experiments or production APIs that auto-scale on demand.Try it outLearn moreDedicated ServerlessGet guaranteed throughput and consistent latency while keeping serverless simplicity — your own isolated pool with predictable performance.Learn moreDedicatedOperate on fully reserved GPUs for maximum control, privacy, and performance — bring custom models or hardware with enterprise-grade reliability.Try it outLearn moreBatchProcess massive datasets or offline jobs up to 80–90% cheaper than real-time inference.Try it outLearn moreTrusted byAI innovatorsAndreas StuhlmüllerCEO, ElicitElicit is using LLMs to screen more than 100,000 scientific papers each day, but the cost of high-quality real-time processing was prohibitive. Parasail was essential for removing this bottleneck. Working with Mike and the Parasail team has been refreshingly straightforward — they’re responsive, technically excellent, and helped us get high-throughput screening into production with minimal engineering overhead. We’re already exploring the next use case for their platform.”Alan NicholCo-Founder & CTO, RasaWe needed to deploy our custom model quickly and cost-effectively. Parasail got us up and running in no time. Their team responded immediately to our request for lower latency in Europe, setting up an endpoint that improved user experience for our customers. The economics were so favorable that we could make our tutorial model publicly accessible for free without asking customers to enter API keys or credit cards.Oussama ElachqarCo-Founder, OumiParasail’s batch processing made it significantly easier for us to generate millions of responses for dataset building and researching. Running large batches of requests allowed us to easily coordinate access among our researchers and saved us tremendous time and effort compared to handling millions of individual requests with retries and rate limitations. It’s been a seamless experience that enabled us to move faster.Shawn LewisCo-Founder and CTO, Weights & BiasesParasail moved at lightning speed to get us set up with massive DeepSeek capacity and top-shelf throughput. websiteContent.mainContent: copyCopychevron-downWelcomeParasail provides businesses with affordable, high-performance cloud GPUs to run demanding AI tasks without costly hardware investments. By aggregating top AI hardware providers, Parasail offers scalable, on-demand access to powerful computing resources, simplifying infrastructure management. This enables companies of all sizes to deploy advanced AI solutions efficiently, focus on innovation, and access cutting-edge technology typically reserved for large-scale operations.Provides Cost-Effective Access to Powerful AI Hardware: Parasail offers businesses access to high-performance cloud GPUs, allowing them to run demanding AI and data-processing tasks affordably without investing in expensive hardware. This is particularly beneficial for companies working with large, complex models that typically require costly and sophisticated hardware setups.Simplifies AI Infrastructure: Parasail aggregates AI hardware providers, offering an easy-to-use platform that minimizes the need for lengthy contracts, complex setup, and data migration. This lets companies focus on developing and deploying AI solutions rather than managing infrastructure.Enhances AI Model Efficiency and Scalability: Companies can leverage Parasail’s hardware for various workloads, including real-time processing, large batch jobs, and continuous tasks, to boost efficiency and performance. This flexibility supports both small-scale and enterprise-grade AI applications.Enables Innovation and Accessibility in AI: By lowering costs and simplifying access, Parasail democratizes cutting-edge AI technologies, making advanced AI capabilities accessible to companies of all sizes, not just major tech players.hashtagJump right inServerlessStart using our on-demand serverless modelsDedicatedSpin up your own model with the GPUs you wantBatchCreate a batch job to run large workloadshashtagCookbooksChat CompletionsStart creating chatsRAGEnrich and create vector dataMulti-modelHandle images, audio, video and moreNextServerlesschevron-rightLast updated 3 months ago

websiteContent.title: Dedicated Endpoints | Parasailbarssearchcircle-xmarkchevron-upchevron-downgitbookxmarkblock-quotechevron-downcopychevron-downhashtaghashtagarrow-up-righthashtaghashtagchevron-leftchevron-right
websiteContent.mainContent: copyCopychevron-downDedicated InstanceDedicated EndpointsDedicated endpoints are private endpoints that you can in a few clicks run most transformers on HuggingFace on our full range of GPU offerings at the best prices on the market. Dedicated endpoints are ideal for creating production endpoints with full control over performance and QOS, and for experimenting with models that are not available on public serverless endpoints.hashtagAdvantages of Dedicated Endpoints:Great for both experimenting and deploying to production: run most transformers on HuggingFace by simply entering the URL.Run custom models hosted in your private HuggingFace repo by simply entering an access token.Select from a variety of hardware types—4090 s up to H200 s—to match your cost and performance needs.Scale your instance up as traffic grows with load balanced replicas.Hit aggressive latency targets through methods like GPU selection, model quantization, and speculative decoding.Great Customer Value: Our dedicated model service delivers great value to customers through easy on-demand usage for even large scale deployments, no contracts or commitments, and the lowest hourly rate GPUs anywhere. You can pause and resume models at any time and billing is only activated when the model is active.Model Compatibility: Behind the scenes, we work with a variety of inference stacks to maintain the best model compatibility and performance. Depending on how a model is launched, if it is coordinated with the community (for example Llama models), we will have 0-day support. Sometimes if models are dropped unannounced it can take a few days to a week to get support in. If a model is released and we do not offer support please let us know on our Discord [LINK] or through our support channels and we will investigate.We currently support most transformers, while support for diffusion models and more conventional AI models like CNNs or LSTMs are further down our roadmap. Some models are also not supported because they contain local code execution in the model repo, however we are able to whitelist trusted model providers so please contact us if you would like us to investigate a model support issue.hashtagCreating a Dedicated EndpointAfter clicking on the Dedicated Tab, the configuration page will be shown:Deployment Name:Guide me (deprecated): this is a feature being deprecated that allowed for a click-through selection of popular AI models.HuggingFace ID / URL: this is where you specify the model to load. Currently we only support models hosted either publicly or privately on HuggingFace. If your model is local, its very easy to load it into a private HuggingFace repo. For public models, you can specify either the URL or the model ID.For example, for the FP8 version of Qwen 2 72 B Instruct, you can specify:https://huggingface.co/neuralmagic/Qwen2-72B-Instruct-FP8arrow-up-right or neuralmagic/Qwen2-72B-Instruct-FP8FYI HuggingFace has a convenient copy button next to the model ID:HuggingFace Token: this allows you to load a model hosted on a private HuggingFace repo. This is ideal for custom models fine-tuned on private data. For more information on generating a token, please read Deploying private models through HuggingFace Repos.If the model is supported, you'll get a green message. If the model is not supported, please contact us on Discord or through your support channel and we will investigate.After you select your model, you can select a hardware service tier. In closed beta, we pool the hardware into low-cost, low-latency, and ultra-low-latency. As we approach launch, we will support the selection of specific GPU instances such as H100 s and H200 s. Once you are ready, hit the deploy button and the endpoint will be live. If you want to set advanced options, click the advanced dropdown.Once the model is deployed, you will be taken to the Dedicated Status Page. If the model weights have been cached previously, the model will take 3-5 minutes to start. If the model has to be downloaded from HuggingFace, it could take anywhere from 10 minutes to multiple hours to download the weights.hashtagAdvanced OptionsSpeculative Decoding Draft Model Name: This allows you to set a draft model for faster output token speeds with speculative decoding. This is an advanced concept that requires some care, so please read this guide before using: Speeding up Dedicated Models with Speculative DecodingReplicas: This spins up multiple replicas that are automatically load balanced. This is designed for high throughput production deployments and the hourly cost will multiply by the number of replicas.Model Mode: Most models will autodetect, however some models support multiple modes from the same model (such as GritLM, which can be both a generative and embedding model). If the autodetect creates the wrong type of endpoint, this can be used to force a specific mode.Scale Down Policy: If you are using this model for experimenting or R&D, we highly recommend a scaledown policy to shut the model down after a certain amount of time. We've heard plenty of horror stories from users of other providers who rack up big bills from a forgotten endpoint so this is highly recommended.Max concurrent connections per replicas: If the number of concurrent requests equals this amount, the endpoint will return a 429 Too Many Requests error until there is free space. This is necessary to prevent too many users from overloading an endpoint and drastically reducing quality of service.hashtagDedicated Status PageOnce a dedicated model is deployed, you can control and view the metrics of a deployment on the status page.The Pause button will temporarily deactivate the endpoint and pause billing. You can also Edit the model settings and redeploy. The Destroy button will permanently delete the endpointPreviousComing Soonchevron-leftNextSpeeding up Dedicated Models with Speculative Decodingchevron-rightLast updated 3 months ago

headings: hashtagGetting Started with the Parasail Batch Helper Library
websiteContent.mainContent: copyCopychevron-downBatchQuick startThis page gives an introduction to Parasail's Batch Processing Library. For more detailed information, jump to:Using the Batch UIParasail's Python Batch Helper Library ReferenceBatch file format (100% compatible with OpenAI)OpenAI Batch API reference (Parasail is 100% compatible)Parasail's batch processing engine is a straightforward and inexpensive way to process thousands or millions of LLM inferences. Batch inferencing is easy: create an input file, start a batch job, wait for it to finish, then download the output.OpenAI compatibility, Parasail batch helper libraryhashtagGetting Started with the Parasail Batch Helper Librarycircle-infoYou can find the Parasail Batch Helper Library on Github. This library is 100% compatible with both Parasail and OpenAI https://github.com/parasail-ai/openai-batcharrow-up-rightThe first step is to create a Parasail API key: https://www.saas.parasail.io/keysarrow-up-right. This key should be stored in your environment through something like a .bashrc file or a .env file, or passed through the command line invocation. Pasting directly in code isn't recommended as keys can get leaked.Next, install openai-batch, the batch helper library that's compatible both with Parasail and OpenAI:Copypip install openai-batchNow you're ready to run a batch job in as little as five lines of code. The batch endpoint supports most transformers on Hugging Face—all you have to do is put the Hugging Face ID in the request. There's no need for this model to be a dedicated or endpoint. In this example, Parasail uses NousResearch/DeepHermes-3-Mistral-24 B-Preview (https://huggingface.co/NousResearch/DeepHermes-3-Mistral-24B-Previewarrow-up-right).Copy#test_batch.py import random from openai_batch import Batch # Create a batch with random prompts with Batch() as batch: objects = ["cat", "robot", "coffee mug", "spaceship", "banana"] for i in range(100): batch.add_to_batch( model="NousResearch/DeepHermes-3-Mistral-24B-Preview", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"Tell me a joke about a {random.choice(objects)}"}] ) #temporary workaround file_path = batch.submission_input_file.name batch.submission_input_file.close() batch.submission_input_file = file_path batch._should_close = False # Submit, wait for completion, and download results result, output_path, error_path = batch.submit_wait_download() print(f"Batch completed with status {result.status} and stored in {output_path}")This code looks for PARASAIL_API_KEY in the environment, and passing it through the command line is straightforward. Running this code produces the following output:The first two lines of batch-itvt3wmjs7-output.jsonl are the prompt responses:hashtagBatch Submission LimitationsBoth Parasail and OpenAI limit the maximum size of the batch input files:Up to 50,000 requests (lines)Up to 500 MB total input file size for ParasailUp to 250 MB total input file size for OpenAIMax completion tokens for Parasail defaults to 8,192, but can be overridden to 16,384 through the max_completion_tokens parameter.Workloads that exceed these limits must be split into multiple batches. The add_to_batch function in the Parasail Batch Helper Libraryarrow-up-right will raise a ValueError exception when the file size or request count is exceeded for the provider.hashtagResuming Batch JobsA major convenience of batch processing is the ability to submit a job and resume the monitoring in a different process or flow. A developer can upload hundreds of batch jobs and millions of prompts without worrying about program crashes, errors, or resets—those prompts are processed on Parasail's servers until successful completion.Changing the last two lines of the previous example submits the job and prints out the batch ID, then exits.With a batch ID of batch-tclfzwczcd, you can now wait for the batch to finish and download it in a separate script:Which outputs:hashtagBroad Model and Parameter Supportcircle-infoChat completions, embeddings, multimodal inputs, the full range of prompt parameters, and even OpenAI models are supportedOpenAI ModelshashtaghashtagEmbedding ModelsGritLM—developed by Contextual and hosted on Hugging Face by Parasail—and GTE-Qwen2-7 B-Instruct from Alibaba are two excellent open source embedding models that rival proprietary models. Embeddings like these can be easily run by changing messagesto input .parasail-ai/GritLM-7 B-vllmarrow-up-rightAlibaba-NLP/gte-Qwen2-7 B-instructarrow-up-rightAs seen below, Parasail strongly recommends using base64 encodingarrow-up-right encoding_format="base64" to reduce the size of the output files for both Parasail and OpenAI.Parametersadd_to_batch supports all of the parameters that client.chat.completions.create or client.embedding.create supports, though note that open source models on parasail may not always support every parameter.MetadataYou can add metadata to the batch. This is useful for passing information between separate submit and download processes, as well as tracking the results on the status UI page. Any metadata you add to the submission is visible in the Batch UI progress section. For detailed information about the metadata field, see the OpenAI Batch API Submit Specificationarrow-up-right.hashtagParasail Batch UIThe Parasail Batch UI can be used to upload new batches, track the status of batches, and download results when finished.While a batch is queued or running, you can view its status, download the input, or cancel it:When a batch job is finished, you can download the input and the output file as well as view the total token usage:Batch SubmissionBatches can be submitted directly from the Batch section of the platform by clicking the Create Batch button. This brings up a dialog to upload a JSONL file.Below is an example JSONL file, which follows the OpenAI format for batch submissions. Unlike the Parasail Batch Helper Library, this doesn't support OpenAI models and only supports open source Hugging Face transformers and embeddings.file-download2KBbatch-input-example.jsonldownloadDownloadarrow-up-right-from-squareOpenFurther ReadingParasail's Python Batch Helper Library ReferenceBatch file format (100% compatible with OpenAI)OpenAI Batch API reference (Parasail is 100% compatible)PreviousAuto-Scalingchevron-leftNextBatch Processing with Private Modelschevron-rightLast updated 16 days ago

websiteContent.mainContent: Privacy PolicyLast Updated: November 10, 2025Welcome to Parasail! We’re glad you’re here. This Privacy Policy (this “Policy”) describes how Parasail, Inc. (“Parasail”, “we”, or “us”) collects, uses, discloses, and otherwise processes personal data (as defined below) in connection our on-demand GPU inference services located at www.parasail.io, saas.parasail.io, and api.parasail.io (collectively, our “Platform”), and related content, services, products, and other functionality offered on or through the Platform (collectively, our “Services”). We have implemented this Policy because your privacy is important to us.1. Personal DataFor the purposes of this Policy, “personal data” means any data or information that identifies, relates to, describes, is capable of being associated with, or could reasonably be linked, directly or indirectly, with a particular natural person or household, or any other data or information that constitutes “personal data”, “personal information”, or “personally identifiable information” under applicable law.2. Personal Data We Collect from You DirectlyWe will collect any personal data that you choose to share with us, or that you allow us to access as part of creating an account on the Platform or otherwise using the Service. This may include the following:Personal data associated with creating and updating your user account(s) on the Platform. This may include things like email address and name. We use this information to administer your account, make the Platform and the Service available to you, communicate with you about your account, the Platform, and the Service, and for customer support purposes.Personal data associated with collecting and/or processing payments. This may include things like name and address. Please note that we use third party payment processors, including Stripe, to process credit card payments made to us. We do not retain any personally identifiable financial information related to credit card payments, such as credit card numbers. Instead, you will provide all such information directly to the third party processor. The processor’s use of your personal data is governed by their own privacy policy.Personal data associated with receiving and responding to inquiries or communications from you. This may include things like email, name, and any other personally identifiable data you choose to include in your communications with us. We use this information to investigate and respond to your inquiries and/or to communicate with you, and to enhance the Platform and the Service.Personal data associated with receiving and responding to support requests. This may include things like email, name, and any other personally identifiable data you choose to include in your communications with us. We use this information to provide customer support to you.Personal data associated with receiving marketing communications from us. To the extent that you agree to receive marketing communications from us, you may provide us with things like your email, name, and other personally identifiable information about yourself. We use this information to manage our communications with you, and to send you information about products and services that we think may be of interest to you. If you want to stop receiving email messages or other marketing communications from us, click the “unsubscribe” link at the bottom of the email/marketing communication. Note that you cannot unsubscribe from certain Platform-related or Service-related communications (e.g., account verification, confirmations, technical notices, or legal notices).Personal data associated with feedback you choose to provide to us. This may include things like email, name, and any other personally identifiable data you choose to include in your communications with us. We will use this information to communicate with you, to conduct market research, inform our marketing and advertising activities, and otherwise improve the Platform and the Service.Personal data associated with applying for a job with us. This may include things like your contact information, demographic information, educational and work history, information you provide to us during the course of any interviews, and any other information you choose to provide to us during the course of applying for a job with us. We will use this information to as part of the process of determining whether you are a good fit to work with us at Parasail.Device and Usage Information. We may collect data about your device, and how you and your device interact with the Platform and/or the Service. For example, we may collect usage data (including things like the features of the Platform/Service you choose to use, the web pages you visit, your interactions on our website(s), and your interactions with us via email or other methods), and device, connectivity, and configuration data (including things like information about your device, the network you use to connect to the Platform/Service, data about your operating system and other software installed on your device, IP address, browser type, and referring URLs). We use this information to improve the Platform and the Service.Metadata. We may collect certain metadata – essentially, data about your data – that could be used to identify you. This may include things like statistics related to your usage of the Platform/Service (e.g., length of engagement, location, and browser type), your usage of the API token that you use to access the Platform/Service, and your large language model (LLM) token usage and time usage (e.g., what models you choose to leverage, and how may tokens you consume). We use this information to improve the Platform and the Service.3. Personal Data We Receive About You from Third PartiesWe will collect certain personal data from third parties, which we often combine with personal data we collect from you. This may include the following:Information we receive from authentication services you use to connect to the Platform or the Service. This may include things like OIDC issuer ID (e.g., GoogleID, Auth0 ID, Okta). These services will authenticate your identity and provide you with the option to share certain personal data with us, which could include your name, email address, and other information. The specific data we receive will vary based on that third party’s policies, and your privacy settings on that third party site. We use this information to administer your account, make the Platform and the Service available to you, communicate with you about your account, the Platform, and the Service, and for customer support purposes.4. How We Use the Personal Data We CollectWe use personal data to provide and improve the Platform and the Service, and to perform essential business operations. This includes operating the Services, maintaining and improving the performance of the Services, developing new features, conducting research, and providing customer support. Examples of such uses include the following:Making the Platform and Service Available to You. We use data to carry out your transactions with us, and to make the Platform and the Service available to you. In certain cases, the Platform and/or Service may include personalized features and recommendations that enhance your productivity and enjoyment, and automatically tailor your experience based on the data we have about you.Technical support. We use data to diagnose product problems, and to provide other customer care and support services.Improving the Platform and the Service. We use data to continually improve our Platform and our Service, including system administration, system security, and adding new features or capabilities. Business Operations. We use data to develop aggregate analyses and business intelligence that enable us to operate, protect, make informed decisions, and report on the performance of our business.Recruitment. If you apply for a job with us, we use your data for recruitment purposes.Promotions. We may use your data to administer promotions, surveys, or other similar activities.Improving Advertising Campaigns. We may use your data to improve our advertising campaigns, primarily in an effort to prevent targeting of impressions via third-party channels when they are not relevant to you.Sending Periodic Emails. We may use your data to send you periodic emails. Depending on the marketing preferences you select when you create your account, we may send you occasional marketing emails about our products and services, which you can unsubscribe from at any time using the link provided in the message.Generally. We use data to respond to your inquiries and requests relating to our Platform and Service, to create and administer your account, to process payments, and to provide us with information and access to resources that you have requested from us. We also use data for general business purposes, including, among other things, to improve customer service, to help us improve the content and functionality of the Platform and the Service, to better understand our users, to protect against wrongdoing, to enforce our Terms of Service, and to generally manage our business.Communications. We use data we collect to communicate with you, and to personalize our communications with you. For example, we may contact you to inform you when your subscription is ending, to discuss your account, to let you know when updates are available, to remind you about features of the Platform and the Service that are available for your use, to update you about a support request, or to invite you to participate in a survey. Additionally, you can sign up for email subscriptions, and choose whether you want to receive marketing communications from us.5. We Do Not Use Personal Data Contained in Your Input or Output to Train Our Models, or to Improve the Platform or Service.We treat personal data that is contained within Input and Output differently than in the rest of this Policy.For the purposes of this Policy, “Input” means any data, images, code, or other content (including, without limitation, text, graphics, audio files, video files, or computer software) that you either: (i) publish, upload to, or use in conjunction with the Platform; (ii) make available in conjunction with the Platform, or (iii) allow the Platform to access; and “Output” means content and/or data generated by the Platform, and/or one or more model(s), in response to a query.We will not use your Input, or your Output, or any personal data contained in that Input or Output, to train our own models or to improve the Platform or the Service.6. CookiesCookies are small files that a site or its service provider transfers to your computer’s hard drive through your web browser (if you allow it) that enables the site’s or service provider’s systems to recognize your browser and capture and remember certain information. You can choose to disable cookies, but if you do, your ability to use or access certain parts of the Platform or Service may be affected.We use cookies and other similar identifiers to understand and save your preferences for future visits, to advertise to you on other sites, and to compile aggregate data about site traffic and site interaction so that we can offer better site experiences and tools in the future. Some examples of cookies and tracking technology that may be included in the Platform and Service include Google Analytics, PostHog, and Microsoft Clarity.You may refuse to accept cookies by activating the setting on your browser that allows you to refuse the setting of cookies. However, if you choose to disable cookies, you may be unable to access certain parts of our site. A banner asking you to accept our cookies policy will be displayed upon the first visit to our website (or the first visit after you delete your cookies). Unless you have adjusted your browser setting so that it will refuse cookies and/or you have not accepted our cookies policy, our system will issue cookies when you log on to our site.Our web pages may contain electronic images known as web beacons (also called single-pixel gifs) that we use to help deliver cookies on our websites, and to count users who have visited those websites. We may also include web beacons in our promotional email messages or newsletters, to determine whether and when you open and act on them.Finally, our Services may contain web beacons or similar technologies from third-party analytics providers that help us compile aggregated statistics about the effectiveness of our promotional campaigns or other operations. These technologies enable the analytics providers to set or read their own cookies or other identifiers on your device, through which they can collect information about your online activities across applications, websites or other products. However, we prohibit these analytics providers from using web beacons on our sites to collect or access information that directly identifies you (such as your name or email address).7. How We Protect Your Personal DataThe security of your personal data is important to us. Parasail follows generally accepted industry standards, including the use of reasonable and appropriate administrative, physical, and technical safeguards, to protect your personal data. This includes implanting reasonable access controls, and limiting access to systems that contain personal data to those people who have the need to access them (e.g., the “least-privilege” principle). However, no method of transmission over the Internet, or method of electronic storage, is fully secure. Therefore, while Parasail strives to use commercially reasonable means to protect your personal data while in motion and at rest, Parasail cannot and does not guarantee your data’s absolute security or confidentiality.8. When We Share Your Information with OthersWe share your personal data with your consent, or as necessary to make the Platform and the Service available to you. We also share personal data with vendors or agents working on our behalf for the purposes described in this Policy. For example, companies we have hired to provide payment processing services, CRM services, and customer support may need access to personal data to provide those functions. In such cases, these companies are required to abide by our data privacy and security requirements, and are not allowed to use personal data they receive from us for any other purpose. We may disclose your personal data as part of a corporate transaction such as a corporate sale, merger, reorganization, dissolution, or similar event.Finally, we will access, transfer, disclose, and/or preserve personal data, when we have a good faith belief that doing so is necessary to:comply with applicable law or respond to valid legal process, judicial orders, or subpoenas; respond to requests from public or governmental authorities, including for national security or law enforcement purposes;protect the vital interests of our users, customers, or other third parties (including, for example, to prevent spam or attempts to defraud users of our products, or to help prevent the loss of life or serious injury of anyone);operate and maintain the security of the Platform and Service, including to prevent or stop an attack on our computer systems or networks; protect the rights, interests or property of Parasail or third parties; prevent or investigate possible wrongdoing in connection with the Platform of Service; or enforce our Terms of Service.We may use and share aggregated non-personal information with third parties for marketing, advertising, and analytics purposes.We do not sell your personal information to third parties.9. Where We Store and Process Personal DataPersonal data we collect may be stored and processed in the United States, the European Union, or in any other country where Parasail or its affiliates, future subsidiaries or service providers maintain facilities. The storage location(s) are chosen in order to enable efficient operation, improve performance, and create redundancies in order to protect the data in the event of an outage or other problem. We take steps to ensure that the data we collect is processed according to the provisions of this Policy, and the requirements of applicable law wherever the data is located.We transfer personal data from the European Economic Area, the UK and Switzerland to other countries, some of which have not been determined by the European Commission to have an adequate level of data protection. When we engage in such transfers, we use a variety of legal mechanisms to help ensure your rights and protections travel with your data, including standard contractual clauses that have been approved by the European Commission. 10. How Long We Retain Your InformationWe will retain your personal data for as long as necessary to make the Platform and the Service available to you, or for other essential purposes such as complying with our legal obligations, resolving disputes, and enforcing our agreements. Because these needs can vary for different types of data in the context of the different aspects of the Platform and the Service, actual retention periods can vary significantly. The criteria we use to determine the retention periods include:How long is the personal data needed to make the Platform and/or Service available, and/or operate our business? This includes such things such as maintaining and improving the performance of the Platform and the Service, keeping our systems secure, and maintaining appropriate business and financial records. This is the general rule that establishes the baseline for most data retention periods.Is there an automated control, such as in your privacy dashboard, that enables you to access and delete the personal data at any time? If there is not, a shortened data retention time will generally be adopted.Is the personal data of a sensitive type? If so, a shortened retention time would generally be appropriate.Has the user provided consent for a longer retention period? If so, we will retain the data in accordance with your consent.Is Parasail subject to a legal, contractual, or similar obligation to retain the data? Examples can include mandatory data retention laws in the applicable jurisdiction, government orders to preserve data relevant to an investigation, or data that must be retained for the purposes of litigation.We do not store or log any personal data you send as Input to the “serverless” and “dedicated” versions of the Service, and we will not inspect such data without your permission, and will only retain such data for as long as is necessary to provide the Service to you (i.e., for the time it takes to generate Output and deliver that Output to you). We generally store and log any personal data you send as Input to the “batch” version of the Service for 30 days, though the specific timeframe may vary.11. How You Can Access and Control Your Personal DataYou can view, access, edit, delete, or request a copy of your personal data for certain aspects of the Platform and the Service. You can also make choices about Parasail’s collection and use of certain data. How you can access and control your personal data will depend on which aspects of the Platform and Service you use.You can always choose whether you want to receive marketing communications from us. You can also opt out from receiving marketing communications from us by using the opt-out link on the communication.Data Access and Portability. You can request a copy of your personal data by sending an email to hello@parasail.io with “Please send me a copy of my personal data” in the subject line. Parasail will verify your ability to access that email, then send you a digital export of the data we hold that is associated with your email address. We will use reasonable efforts to respond to your request within 30 days of our receipt of the request. Data Erasure. You can request that Parasail delete your personal data by sending an email to hello@parasail.io with “Please delete my personal data” in the subject line. Parasail will verify your ability to access that email, then delete the personal data associated with your email address. We will use reasonable efforts to respond to your request within 30 days of our receipt of the request. Please note that we retain billing and usage metadata about a company or individual as required for compliance with law and regulation.Data Correction. You can modify your personal data by sending an email to hello@parasail.io with “Please update my personal data” in the subject line. Parasail will verify your ability to access that email, then update the fields where possible within the Services. We will use reasonable efforts to respond to your request within 30 days of our receipt of the request. Your Communications Preferences. You can choose whether you wish to receive marketing communications from us. If you receive marketing communications from us and would like to opt out, you can do so by following the directions in that communication. Alternatively, you can request that we withdraw consent to use your personal data by sending an email to hello@parasail.io with “Please withdraw my consent” in the subject line. Parasail will verify your ability to access that email, then delete the personal data associated with your email address. We will use reasonable efforts to respond to your request within 30 days of our receipt of the request. Please note that these choices do not apply to mandatory communications that are part of the Platform or Service.12. Children’s Personal DataThe Platform and the Service are not directed to, and we do not intend to, or knowingly, collect or solicit personal data from children under the age of 13. If an individual is under the age of 13, they should not use the Platform or the Service, or otherwise provide us with any personal data (either directly, or by any other means). If a child under the age of 13 has provided personal data to us, we encourage the child’s parent or guardian to contact us to request that we remove the personal data from our systems. If we learn that any personal data we have collected has been provided by a child under the age of 13, we will promptly delete that personal data.13. Third Party LinksOccasionally, at our discretion, we may include or offer third party products or services on our website or through the Platform or Service. If you access other websites using the links provided, the operators of these websites may collect information from you that will be used by those websites in accordance with their privacy policies. These third party sites have separate and independent privacy policies. We, therefore, have no responsibility or liability for the content and activities of these linked sites. 14. Changes to this PolicyWe will update this Policy as necessary to reflect customer feedback and changes in our Platform and Service. When we post changes to this Policy, we will revise the "last updated" date at the top of the Policy. If there are material changes to the Policy or in how Parasail will use your personal data, we will notify you either by prominently posting a notice of such changes before they take effect or by sending you a direct notification. We encourage you to periodically review this Policy to learn how Parasail is using your personal data.15. Contact UsIf you have any questions regarding this Policy, or our Platform or Service, please send an email to hello@parasail.ioReady to unlock the power of AI?Join other developers who are already using Parasail to optimize their workloads and cut costs.Get started with free credits today.First NameLast NameEmail AddressPhone NumberYour MessageThank you!Your submission has been received!Oops! Something went wrong while submitting the form.Next generation inference for the AI economy.HomeAbout UsPricingDocsPrivacy PolicyTerms of Service

websiteContent.mainContent: Terms of ServiceLast Updated: November 10, 2025Welcome to Parasail! We’re glad you’re here. These Terms of Service (these “Terms”) describe your rights and responsibilities when using our on-demand GPU inference services located at www.parasail.io, saas.parasail.io, and api.parasail.io (collectively, the “Platform”). Please read these Terms carefully; if you don’t agree to be bound by these Terms, you aren’t allowed to access or use the Platform. 1. Things You Should Know1.1 These Terms Form a Binding ContractUnless the parties have mutually executed a different agreement covering your use of the Platform, these Terms form a binding contract between Customer and Parasail. “We,” “our,” and “us” refers to Parasail, Inc., while “Customer” or “you” refers to you (or, as applicable, to the company or entity that you represent). The “Effective Date” of these Terms is the date that you first use our website or access any part of our Platform. If you access or use the Platform in your capacity as an employee, consultant or agent of a company or other entity, you represent that you are an employee, consultant or agent of that company or entity, and that you have the authority to bind that company or entity to these Terms. 1.2 These Terms Contain an Arbitration ProvisionThese Terms contain an arbitration provision (see Section 12, below). Please review the arbitration provision carefully, since it affects your rights. By using the Platform or any part of it, you understand and agree to be bound by the arbitration provision.1.3 There are Three Ways to Use the PlatformThere are currently three ways to use the Platform: “serverless”, “dedicated”, and “batch” (each, a “Service”). We provide details about each Service in Section 3 below. We may modify Services or add new Services from time to time. 1.4. Making Your Purchasing DecisionsWe may share information about our future product plans with you, because we want to get product feedback from you early and often. Our public statements about those product plans are only an expression of intent – please do not rely on them when making a purchase. If you decide to use our Platform, that decision should be based on the services, functionality and features we have available today, and not on the delivery of any services, functionality or features that may be available in the future.1.5 Beta ProductsOccasionally, we may beta test new features or functionality. These may be identified as “beta,” “pre-release,” or “early access,” or words or phrases with similar meanings (each, a “Beta Product”). Beta Products may not be ready for prime time, so we make them available “as is,” without warranty of any kind, express or implied. Should you encounter any faults with our Beta Products, we would love to hear about them; our primary reason for running beta programs is to iron out issues before making a new feature or function widely available.1.6 We Want Your FeedbackThe more suggestions our customers make, the better the Platform becomes. If you send us any feedback or suggestions regarding the Platform (whether via feedback.parasail.io, or any other way), there is a chance we will use them, so you grant us (on behalf of you, and the entity you represent) an unlimited, irrevocable, perpetual, sublicensable, transferable, royalty-free license to use any such feedback or suggestions for any purpose, without any obligation or compensation to you or your personnel. 1.7 We Can Modify These TermsWe reserve the right to change or modify these Terms, or any of our other policies or guidelines, at any time upon notice to you. We may provide that notice in a variety of ways, including, among other things, sending you an email, posting a notice on the Platform or Service itself, or by posting the revised Terms on our website and revising the date at the top of these Terms. Any changes or modifications will be effective after we provide notice that these Terms have been modified. You acknowledge that your continued use of the Platform following such notice constitutes your acceptance of the modified Terms.1.8 We Can Also Modify the PlatformWe reserve the right — at any time, and without notice or liability to you — to modify the Platform, or any part of it, temporarily or permanently. We may modify the Platform for a variety of reasons, including, among other things, adding new features or Services, implementing new protocols, maintaining compatibility with emerging standards, or complying with regulatory requirements.2. Accessing and Using the Platform 2.1 Access GrantSubject to your continued compliance with these Terms (including, among other things, paying the fees you owe us), Parasail grants you a limited, non-transferable, non-exclusive, revocable right and license to access and use the Platform, solely for your own internal business purposes. 2.2 RestrictionsExcept as expressly authorized by these Terms, you may not: (a) modify, disclose, alter, translate or create derivative works of the Platform; (b) license, sublicense, resell, distribute, lease, rent, lend, transfer, assign or otherwise dispose of the Platform (or any part of it); (c) offer any part of the Platform on a timeshare or service bureau basis; (d) allow or permit any third party to access or use the Platform; (e) use the Platform to store or transmit any viruses, software routines, or other code designed to permit anyone to access in an unauthorized manner, disable, erase or otherwise harm software, hardware, or data, or to perform any other harmful actions; (f) use the Platform to build a competitive product or service, or to copy any features or functions of the Platform (including, without limitation, the look-and-feel of the Platform); (g) interfere with or disrupt the integrity or performance of the Platform; (h) publicly disclose to any third party any performance information or analysis relating to the Platform; (i) remove, alter or obscure any proprietary notices in or on the Platform, including copyright notices; (j) use the Platform or any product thereof for any illegal or unauthorized purpose, or in a manner which violates any laws or regulations in your jurisdiction; (k) reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or otherwise attempt to discover the source code, object code, or underlying structure, ideas, or algorithms that make up the Platform or any software, documentation, or data relating to the Platform, except to the limited extent that applicable law prohibits such a restriction; (l) use any robot, spider, scraper, off-line reader, data mining tool, or any other automated means to access the Platform in a manner than sends more request messages to the servers running the Platform than a human can reasonably produce in the same period of time by using a conventional online web browser; (m) use any content available on or via the Platform (including any caption information, keywords, or other metadata) for any machine learning and/or artificial intelligence training or development purposes, or for any technologies designed or intended for the identification of natural persons; (n) buy, sell, or transfer API keys without our prior written consent in each case; (o) take any action that imposes, or may impose (as determined by us, in our sole discretion), an unreasonable or disproportionately large load on our infrastructure; or (p) cause or permit any third party to do any of the foregoing.2.3 Definitions. Description: The “batch” Service is a way for you to upload large amounts of Customer Inputs, and then query one or more Models against that data. The Platform will help you index and query that data. You will receive the results of the query as Output, which you can then store on your own servers. marketTrends.trend: Shift in AI economics from training-heavy to inference-heavy spend as more workloads move into production (LLM apps, agents, real-time pipelines).
Multi-cloud / cross-cloud frameworks and distributed cloud adoption increase demand for a “unified control plane” across heterogeneous GPU fleets (neo-clouds, alt-HW clouds, colo/tier-2 datacenters).
“Dedicated endpoints” and private inference deployments grow due to sovereignty, privacy, and predictable QoS requirements (drives higher willingness-to-pay for isolation, locality, SLAs).

Hardware diversification: GPUs remain dominant, but inference-optimized silicon (ASIC/TPU/LPU/NPU) expands addressable supply and increases scheduling/portability complexity—favoring abstraction layers.
Latency-sensitive pipelines (voice agents: STT→LLM→TTS; video understanding) drive “inference at the edge/near-edge” and regional POP buildouts; routing and locality become product differentiators.
FinOps for AI emerges: customers increasingly demand token-level unit economics, admission control, caching, batching, quantization, and dynamic routing to minimize $/task while meeting SLOs.

marketDrivers: Explosive growth in public cloud and AI workloads: Gartner forecasts public cloud services reaching $1.42T by 2029, with AI workloads a key driver. ([gartner.com](https://www.gartner.com/en/documents/6302015?utm_source=openai))

Rapid expansion of AI infrastructure investment: Gartner forecasts total AI spending of $2.52T in 2026 and $3.34T in 2027, with AI infrastructure the largest component. ([gartner.com](https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2026-1-15-gartner-says-worldwide-ai-spending-will-total-2-point-5-trillion-dollars-in-2026?utm_source=openai))

Accelerated server buildout for AI: IDC projects AI infrastructure spending to reach $107B by 2028, with cloud deployments comprising the majority. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20241126982316/en/Artificial-Intelligence-Infrastructure-Spending-to-Surpass-the-%24100B-USD-Mark-in-the-Next-5-years-According-to-IDC?utm_source=openai))

AI inference market growth (multiple third-party estimates) indicating a large and expanding spend pool for inference hardware + software + platforms (e.g., $253.75B by 2030). ([grandviewresearch.com](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/artificial-intelligence-ai-inference-market-report?utm_source=openai))
Developers and fast-growing AI-native companies increasingly consume compute as an API (tokens), creating demand for elastic capacity, global routing, and predictable performance.

tamValidation.explanation: Cloud compute TAM claim ($1.5T by 2032): Authoritative forecasts do not commonly publish out to 2032 in public detail, but Gartner’s published research summary indicates public cloud services reach ~$1.42T by 2029, implying that $1.5T by 2032 is plausible and may even be conservative depending on 2029–2032 growth. ([gartner.com](https://www.gartner.com/en/documents/6302015?utm_source=openai)) IDC forecasts $1.35T by 2027 (2023–2027 CAGR 19.9%), also supporting a >$1.5T trajectory into the 2030s (though sustaining ~20% CAGR for a full decade would yield very large numbers, so long-range extrapolations are sensitive). ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20230829368201/en/Worldwide-Spending-on-Public-Cloud-Services-is-Forecast-to-Reach-%241.35-Trillion-in-2027-According-to-New-IDC-Spending-Guide?utm_source=openai)) Inference TAM claim ($365B by 2032 at ~19.2% CAGR): Multiple third-party market studies put 2032 around ~$349B with ~18.9% CAGR (close to the claim), while other reputable research firms publish different CAGRs and endpoints (e.g., ~$253.75B by 2030 at 17.5% CAGR). Because ‘AI inference market’ definitions vary (hardware-only vs hardware+software+services; cloud vs edge; GenAI-only vs all inference), the exact $365B/2032 figure cannot be confirmed as ‘accurate’ without the original methodology, but it is directionally consistent with at least one published 2032 estimate. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/09/24/3155215/0/en/AI-Inference-Market-to-Reach-USD-349-49-Billion-by-2032-Driven-by-Growing-Need-for-Real-Time-Processing-and-Low-Latency-AI-Applications-Research-by-SNS-Insider.html?utm_source=openai)) Claimed ‘rapid growth in 5 months public’: not independently verifiable from market/analyst sources provided here.

marketChallenges: Supply-side volatility and constraints: GPU availability, HBM/memory bottlenecks, and power/cooling limits can cap capacity and raise costs for inference providers.
Hyperscaler competition and bundling: AWS/Azure/GCP can bundle AI infrastructure + managed services, compressing margins for independent inference platforms.

Performance variability across heterogeneous infrastructure (different GPUs/ASICs, networks, datacenter quality) makes consistent QoS/SLA delivery and debugging harder—especially for real-time voice/video pipelines.

Unit economics pressure: token prices are falling in some segments; providers must offset with higher utilization (batching, caching, quantization), better routing, and superior procurement/supply contracts.
Security and compliance overhead for enterprise “dedicated endpoints” (SOC2/ISO, encryption, key management, logging, tenant isolation) increases time-to-revenue and operational cost.

marketGrowthRate.source: Grand View Research — AI Inference Market CAGR 17.5% (2025–2030). ([grandviewresearch.com](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/artificial-intelligence-ai-inference-market-report?utm_source=openai))

marketResearch.regulatoryLandscape: Regulation impacts Parasail primarily through (1) data privacy/sovereignty constraints that shape where inference can run, (2) AI governance obligations that increase enterprise requirements for logging, transparency, and risk controls, and (3) export controls that can restrict access to advanced accelerators in some regions. In the EU, the AI Act entered into force on 1 Aug 2024 and applies on a phased timeline: prohibited AI practices and AI literacy obligations apply from 2 Feb 2025; obligations for general-purpose AI (GPAI) models apply from 2 Aug 2025; most requirements become applicable from 2 Aug 2026; and high-risk AI embedded in regulated products has a longer transition to 2 Aug 2027. ([digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/regulatory-framework-ai?utm_source=openai)) In the US, while not a single binding federal AI law equivalent to the EU AI Act, enterprise adoption is strongly shaped by risk-management expectations (e.g., NIST AI RMF 1.0 and the Generative AI Profile), which in practice can become procurement requirements for enterprises buying dedicated endpoints. ([nist.gov](https://www.nist.gov/publications/artificial-intelligence-risk-management-framework-ai-rmf-10?utm_source=openai)) Export controls and geopolitical rules can change market access for advanced chips (and therefore the composition/location of available supply), creating ongoing compliance and planning risk for a global GPU network. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/bb05a9760abb8a320a447f58599e2ab6?utm_source=openai))

reviews.summary: Product Hunt’s Parasail product page shows a 5.0 rating and '1 review' on the launches page view; however, the publicly rendered reviews section shows 'No reviews yet' (likely due to dynamic rendering or access constraints).
Trustpilot reviews are strongly positive, emphasizing fast inference, competitive token costs, easy OpenAI-spec migration, and responsive support; small sample size (6).

strengths: OpenAI-compatible developer experience (drop-in base_url swap) reduces switching friction from OpenAI-style clients and tooling
Network/aggregation positioning: can source capacity across many providers and geographies (claim of 25+ clouds and 15+ countries) to reduce GPU scarcity risk and improve cost/latency options
Multiple consumption modes match common inference patterns: serverless tokens, batch async, and reserved dedicated endpoints
Dedicated endpoints include practical production controls (replicas/load balancing, pause/resume billing, QoS controls like max concurrent connections per replica)
Clear platform stance against long-term commitments (“no contracts”), aligning with buyer desire to avoid hyperscaler lock-in
weaknesses: Limited third-party software marketplace review coverage (G2 shows 0 reviews; Capterra/TrustRadius listings not found; Product Hunt review visibility inconsistent)
Operational complexity/risk inherent to multi-provider orchestration: performance, availability, and data residency depend on upstream providers and routing decisions
Docs indicate some model support may lag for unannounced releases; some models are unsupported due to local code execution in repos (requires whitelisting/trust process)
Rate limits and GPU quotas exist in practice (e.g., RPM tiers; initial per-org GPU quota), which can conflict with “no limits” marketing for some users
Dedicated endpoint management API recommends using UI first due to limited visibility into GPU performance recommendations and HF compatibility issues

coreFeatures: OpenAI-spec compatible API gateway for chat/completions/embeddings (base URL https://api.parasail.io/v1)
OpenAI Batch API-compatible batch processing (input/output file format compatibility and drop-in client usage with base_url override)
Dedicated private endpoints for Hugging Face transformers (including private HF repos via token) with GPU selection and multi-replica load balancing
Dedicated endpoint lifecycle controls (create/pause/resume/scale/destroy) via UI and REST management API
Performance and cost optimizations (e.g., FP8 quantization support references, speculative decoding guidance, cache-aware batch discounts)
Rate-limit tiers and quota controls (RPM tiers; initial GPU quotas) with upgrade paths
Privacy posture: no storage/logging of serverless/dedicated inputs beyond time needed to return output; batch inputs generally retained ~30 days; no training on customer input/output

marketDynamics.buyerPower: High. Buyers can often switch inference APIs relatively quickly when providers support OpenAI-compatible interfaces, and many workloads are portable across providers. However, buyer power decreases for customers needing strict compliance, bespoke SLAs, or guaranteed capacity, where hyperscalers/neoclouds can lock in via enterprise contracts and reserved capacity.
substitutes: Hyperscaler managed model platforms (AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, Google Vertex AI, Oracle OCI GenAI)
Direct GPU clouds / neoclouds for self-managed inference stacks (CoreWeave, Lambda, Nebius, RunPod)
Alternative inference chips/services (GroqCloud, Cerebras Inference) for supported model sets
Self-hosted inference on owned or colocation GPUs (Kubernetes + vLLM/TGI/SGLang, etc.)
Edge/on-device inference for some workloads (reduces cloud inference spend where feasible)
marketDynamics.entryBarriers: High. The market requires (a) scalable access to scarce high-end accelerators and power-dense data center capacity, (b) deep systems optimization (latency/throughput/cost), (c) strong reliability/security for production APIs, and (d) commercial relationships with many compute suppliers. Industry data indicates GPU-as-a-Service and neocloud segments are growing rapidly, intensifying competition for capacity.
marketDynamics.supplierPower: High. GPU OEMs (especially NVIDIA for top-end accelerators), power/real-estate constrained data center supply, and upstream cloud GPU providers have meaningful leverage. Aggregators like Parasail aim to reduce dependency on any single supplier by routing across many providers, but systemic GPU scarcity and power constraints still propagate through pricing and availability.

portersFiveForces.competitiveRivalry: Very high. Neocloud and inference-platform categories are crowded, growing quickly, and competing on price/performance plus access to the latest hardware.
portersFiveForces.threatOfNewEntrants: Medium. Software-only API layers are easier to launch, but achieving cost/performance leadership and reliable capacity at scale is capital- and partnership-intensive.
portersFiveForces.threatOfSubstitutes: High. Buyers can substitute between hyperscalers, neoclouds, aggregators, and alternative hardware inference services depending on workload requirements.

technicalStack: Kubernetes-based multi-provider orchestration (marketed as “Planetary Kubernetes” control plane)
OpenAI-compatible API surface (base_url override in OpenAI clients)
Hugging Face model loading and compatibility checks for dedicated endpoints
Multiple inference runtimes (docs reference vLLM support for chat/completions endpoints; Parasail states it uses a variety of inference stacks for compatibility/performance)
REST management API for dedicated deployments (control plane API separate from inference API)
productResearch.productDescription: Parasail is an AI inference platform and “AI Deployment Network” that aggregates GPU capacity across a large set of third-party compute providers and routes customer inference workloads over a unified control plane it markets as “Planetary Kubernetes.” It supports (1) serverless, token-priced model APIs via an OpenAI-spec compatible gateway (base URL https://api.parasail.io/v1), (2) OpenAI Batch API-compatible asynchronous batch processing for large volumes of requests, and (3) dedicated private endpoints to deploy most Hugging Face transformers on selected GPU types (from RTX 4090-class up through H200-class), with features including load-balanced replicas, pause/resume billing, and performance optimization techniques such as quantization and speculative decoding. Parasail positions itself as “no limits, no contracts,” aiming to undercut hyperscaler economics by sourcing capacity globally across 15+ countries and 25+ clouds while giving developers near-drop-in migration from OpenAI-compatible clients.
productResearch.competitivePosition: Parasail is positioned as a hybrid: a demand-side inference API (OpenAI-compatible, token-based) plus a supply-side “deployment network” that arbitrages global GPU supply across providers. Versus hyperscalers, Parasail competes on price, faster access to frontier/open models, and reduced procurement friction (no commitments). Versus single-provider neoclouds (CoreWeave, Lambda, Nebius), Parasail’s differentiation is orchestration/aggregation across many providers and offering an opinionated inference product surface (serverless + OpenAI Batch compatibility + managed dedicated endpoints) rather than primarily selling raw GPU instances. The key strategic bet is that routing and pooling fragmented GPU supply can deliver sustainably lower costs and better availability than any single cloud while keeping integration friction minimal through OpenAI compatibility.

teamMembers.bio: Over a decade of AI product leadership as founder and CEO of Mythic, a top VC-backed AI company and pioneer of delivering AI to the edge in a cost-efficient HW and SW platform. We invented new compute technology to unlock powerful capabilities in customer's products and set off an industry-wide movement. My mission is to continue my growth as a leader in the AI space and leverage my strong product mindset to serve customers facing the massive opportunities and perilous pitfalls of AI disruption.

linkedinData.headline: CEO of Parasail - Bringing AI to everyone, everywhere
education: Virginia Tech - Ph.D, Electrical and Computer Engineering
Virginia Tech - M.S., Electrical and Computer Engineering
Virginia Tech - B.S., Electrical and Computer Engineering

experienceDetails.description:
I founded Parasail to rethink the AI cloud and help AI-native companies move as fast as possible

Legacy cloud providers weren’t built for AI. They rely on rigid contracts, limited availability, and massive hidden costs. Parasail is changing that.

We’ve built the first AI Deployment Network—giving teams access to the largest on-demand GPU network in the world. No quotas, no contracts, no complexity. Just the freedom to run and scale any model, anywhere, with full control over cost and performance.

We are building an open compute economy to power the AI revolution. It’s essential that AI doesn’t become a walled garden controlled by a few dominant players. The future of AI must be an open playing field—top to bottom.

experienceDetails.location: Mountain View, California, United States · On-site

experienceDetails.description:
Mythic is the pioneer of power- and cost-efficient AI platform, enabled by breakthrough analog compute technology. Mythic raised $165M and delivered the world’s first production-ready analog AI hardware and software stack to customers, setting off an industry-wide compute-in-memory movement. This role showcases a proven track record of identifying emerging technology trends, taking high-reward risks on new approaches, building a strong team, and leading product execution with relentless determination. 

Mythic: Venture-Funded Years  (2017-2023)
	- Organically built a world-class leadership team and staff of > 150 engineers across a unique range of technical disciplines to successfully transition analog compute to mass production.  
	- Raised $165M from top investors including BlackRock, Softbank, and DFJ. Secured corporate investors and strategic partners, including Micron, Lockheed Martin, IQT, DoD, and Cirrus Logic.
	- Led product and technology development, directly overseeing 4 VP-level leaders; navigated massive R&D and manufacturing challenges, while relentlessly focusing on velocity and accountability. 
	- Worked closely with the business team to build go-to-market strategy, focusing on high-end computer vision market.  Built $300M world-wide sales funnel.
	- Successfully established industry thought leadership and brand identity. Successes include two YouTube videos with > 9M views, articles in top industry news, and analog compute prominently featured in industry roadmaps.
	- Transitioned company to CTO in 2023 to focus on development of next-gen IP. 

Mythic: Government-Funded Years    (2012-2017)
	- Started Mythic after graduate school by winning a $150k DoD research grant, later expanding through relentless evangelizing within the DoD to $2.5M in grants to fund early R&D of Mythic’s core technology. Built core R&D team of 5 and proved the concepts of analog compute. Was technical and hands-on with chip, software, and AI algorithms during this time.

Built core R&D team of 5 and proved the concepts of analog compute. Was technical and hands-on with chip, software, and AI algorithms during this time.

As founder and CEO of Swift Navigation, I am dedicated to helping Swift achieve its mission to enable a future of autonomous vehicles to navigate and understand the world. 

Our precision localization platform improves positioning accuracy from 4 meters to 4 centimeters, supporting a range of autonomous and effective navigation solutions. We are helping over 7,000 customers build the next generation of vehicles and devices. 

My role at Swift is simple; I hire the best people, point them in the right direction, and support them in their achievements. Be a part of our team at https://www.swiftnav.com/join-us
